title: Prove and canonicalize import binding identity across forms
description: Dependency aliasing and accessor lookup appear to omit default/named/namespace form and original Haxe declaration identity. First add an executable same-path/same-name fixture; if it reproduces, obtain the required narrow architecture review before changing the shared identity.
acceptance_criteria: A focused package fixture proves or falsifies the predicted collapse. If reproduced, the reviewed canonical binding identity keeps request coalescing separate, emits exact default/named/namespace bindings in both profiles, keeps declarations/type access aligned, and passes full CI.
notes:
Oracle's reviewed model was implemented as separate request, export-selector, requested-local, typed-origin, and projection-local allocation facts. Exact origin lookup now drives genes-ts, classic JS, and classic declarations; default, named, namespace, repeated, explicitly aliased, and dotted-member imports are covered by one executable fixture. The stricter lookup also exposed a separate hidden error: inferred enum type syntax had resolved tink.Error as JavaScript's global Error. Dependency planning now retains that typed enum result and the full fixture asserts the exact import.

Verification: yarn test:binding-identity; package shapes; side-effect import evidence; classic declarations; source maps; output quality/transaction; TS 5/6/7; Haxe 4.3.7; both todoapp Playwright profiles; all ts2hx gates; and full pinned Node 20 yarn test:ci (551.39s) passed.

Intentionally deferred experiments are tracked separately: imported @:native collisions (genes-ccm), non-core abstracts/static-field origin stability across Haxe lanes (genes-3np), and attribute conflicts across different explicit aliases (genes-ozb).
close_reason: Canonical ESM binding identity is implemented and proven across both output profiles, declarations, runtime behavior, and full CI.

title: Plan and validate the ts2hx source namespace
description: ts2hx independently sanitizes source filenames into Haxe module names and does not validate project-wide package/FQN/output-path uniqueness. Colliding sources can overwrite one staged Haxe file while translation reports success.
acceptance_criteria: One immutable pre-emission source namespace plan validates base-package and directory segments, module FQNs, and output paths; all consumers reuse it; collisions fail deterministically in strict and assisted modes with the prior tree unchanged; focused and full ts2hx gates pass.
close_reason: Implemented and verified one immutable pre-emission source namespace plan. Invalid or colliding package/module/output identities now fail deterministically in strict and assisted modes before publication; all namespace consumers reuse the plan. Focused ts2hx tests and full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Give output entrypoints collision-resistant transaction identities
description: Output transaction manifests and stage directories currently use an extension-free, lossy sanitized stem. Distinct configured output identities can therefore share ownership and stale-delete one another.
acceptance_criteria: Distinct colliding entrypoint names in one directory receive exact versioned owner identities and isolated manifests/stages; sequential rebuilds preserve both trees; ambiguous legacy manifests are never consumed destructively; transaction gates pass.
close_reason: Reproduced the lossy v1 scope with punctuation-colliding entrypoints, then introduced exact basename-plus-extension owner identities in manifest v2 with full SHA-256 filesystem scopes. The transaction now rejects mismatched owner records before publication and preserves ambiguous v1 manifests. Verified by yarn test:output-transaction, yarn test:dual-output, yarn test:genes-ts:sourcemaps, yarn test:versions, and full yarn test:ci (406.37s).

title: Protect unowned legacy StdTypes source maps
description: Genes currently retires StdTypes.ts.map by filename even when no prior Genes manifest owns it. Replace filename-only deletion with affirmative ownership evidence and preserve unowned same-named files.
acceptance_criteria: A no-manifest sentinel StdTypes.ts.map remains byte-identical after a TS build; recognized prior Genes ownership can retire the actual legacy artifact; output transaction and dual-output gates pass.
notes:
Fail-first evidence: the new first-build sentinel reproduced the defect on the old implementation; the build deleted an unowned StdTypes.ts.map and the harness failed with ENOENT.
Fix: removed filename-only retirement. Successful publication now deletes a legacy map only when a recognized prior Genes manifest affirmatively owned that exact path.
Verification: yarn test:output-transaction; yarn test:dual-output; and full yarn test:ci (336.64s) passed. Full CI used writable npm/Playwright caches and an unsandboxed browser process because the managed macOS sandbox blocks Chromium Mach-port registration.
Deferred: collision-resistant identity for distinct entrypoint transactions remains owned by genes-552.
close_reason: Manifest ownership is now the sole cleanup authority; fail-first, dual-output, and full CI evidence are green.

title: Group nullish helper expressions before classic null comparisons
description: OpenCodeHX classic ESM emitted Undefinable.orNull() != null as metadata ?? null != null. JavaScript parses that as metadata ?? false, so a present object escaped where Haxe promised Bool. Promote the existing TS-only nullish-comparison precedence rule to a shared semantic fact consumed by classic and TS.
acceptance_criteria: Present and absent Undefinable object values compared after orNull() produce true and false respectively in both output modes; generated expressions group nullish coalescing before equality; full yarn test:ci passes.
notes: Moved the TS-only hidden-nullish comparison precedence fact into TypeUtil and consumed it from both emitters. Added present/absent Undefinable object runtime regression. OpenCodeHX full classic profile subsequently passed. Full yarn test:ci passed in 302.88s.
close_reason: Shared nullish-comparison grouping and paired dual-output runtime fixture are green; full CI passed.

title: Emit raw-syntax placeholder expressions through classic Genes accessors
description: OpenCodeHX classic ESM exposed that js.Syntax.code("await {0}", moduleLevelFunction(...)) delegates placeholder substitution to the stock Haxe JS stringifier. It prints the flat internal identifier even though Genes imports the module field container, causing a runtime ReferenceError. Reduce this to a generic module-level function fixture and make classic raw-syntax placeholders use Genes expression/accessor emission while retaining template text and TS behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A cross-module Haxe module-level function used inside genes.js.Async.await or metadata await emits a valid imported accessor in classic ESM and precise TypeScript; paired runtime assertions pass; full yarn test:ci passes.
notes: Implemented shared js.Syntax.code placeholder expansion in ExprEmitter; classic now resolves module-level function calls through imported module-field accessors. Added AsyncAwaitHelper/TestAsyncAwait runtime regression. OpenCodeHX classic app progressed past file/tool/formatter async paths. Full yarn test:ci passed in 302.88s.
close_reason: Shared placeholder lowering and paired dual-output runtime fixture are green; full CI passed.

title: Preserve awaited-expression precedence in classic JS
description: A downstream same-source classic profile exposed that property access on an awaited Promise emits with the wrong precedence in classic JS, changing semantics while genes-ts correctly parenthesizes it. Add a generic paired fixture, preserve the awaited expression boundary in shared async lowering, run full CI, and keep both TS and classic output green.
close_reason: Shared raw-syntax receiver planning now preserves awaited property/index semantics in TS and classic JS; paired runtime fixture and full CI pass.

title: Preserve import attributes in classic ESM output
description: Classic Genes recognizes genes.ts.Imports/defaultImportWith dependencies but drops genes.importAttributeType metadata while printing ESM imports. A JSON default import therefore becomes a bare import and fails on modern Node with ERR_IMPORT_ATTRIBUTE_MISSING. Fix this generically in shared dependency/import planning and the classic printer, with a same-source TS/classic fixture and runtime evidence.
acceptance_criteria: One Haxe resource-import fixture emits the JSON import attribute in both genes-ts and classic ESM profiles; classic output executes successfully on the supported Node lanes; ordinary imports remain unchanged; full genes CI passes.
close_reason: Classic and TypeScript printers now consume shared import-attribute grouping, the same-source JSON fixture executes in both profiles, and full yarn test:ci passes.

title: Make ts2hx fail closed on unsupported source
description: ts2hx can silently omit whole source files or unsupported top-level statements and still exit successfully. Introduce structured translation outcomes so every input file and top-level statement is either emitted or diagnosed, with strict mode returning nonzero and no behavior-changing placeholder presented as success.
design: First land the failure contract around the existing emitter: structured diagnostics with source spans, explicit strict versus assisted policy, deterministic exit codes, and transactional output. Preserve existing supported snapshots. A semantic IR remains follow-up work rather than being invented inside this fix.
acceptance_criteria: No source file is skipped through if-not-emitted continue without a diagnostic; unsupported top-level statements fail strict mode; output is not partially committed on strict failure; diagnostics are deterministic and machine-readable; ts2hx tests and root yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: ts2hx now gives every root file an explicit disposition, diagnoses every unsupported top-level statement, removes unsupported throw placeholders, preserves prior output on strict failure, writes deterministic assisted manifests/markers transactionally, and exposes CLI exit codes 0/1/2/3. yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test and full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Restore precise classic Null declarations
description: Classic Genes declaration emission maps Haxe Null<T> to any, regressing vanilla null unions and weakening strict consumers. Restore precise nullable unions and add a strict external consumer gate so runtime-only tests cannot mask declaration regressions.
design: Reuse the existing TypeEmitter null-union logic in both TypeScript and classic declaration profiles while preserving the genes.ts.no_null_union override only where explicitly supported. Add a generated classic declaration consumer with skipLibCheck false and an expected rejection for assigning Map.get directly to a non-null value.
acceptance_criteria: Classic haxe.Constraints.IMap.get emits V | null or null | V rather than any; strict consumer accepts nullable use and rejects non-null assignment; npm test, the declaration consumer, and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Classic Null<T> declarations now emit nullable unions and a strict NodeNext external consumer passes; yarn test:ci passed.

title: Emit complete closed TypeScript interfaces
description: Generated TypeScript interfaces currently receive an unconditional string index signature returning any. That masks DCE-trimmed public members and arbitrary consumer typos. Capture declared public interface fields before generator/DCE mutation, emit the complete closed surface, and reserve index signatures for explicit indexable source constructs.
design: Introduce a narrow public-interface surface cache at the existing onAfterTyping seam and consume it in TsModuleEmitter. Do not add a downstream special case or a general compiler rewrite. Add a TypeScript consumer that requires the full IMap surface and proves an unknown member is rejected.
acceptance_criteria: Generated haxe.Constraints.IMap includes get, set, exists, remove, keys, iterator, keyValueIterator, copy, toString, and clear; ordinary interfaces emit no catch-all index signature; a @ts-expect-error consumer proves unknown members are rejected; focused genes-ts full tests and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Complete pre-DCE public interface capture now emits closed, full IMap surfaces; negative TypeScript consumer rejects unknown members; yarn test:ci passed.

title: genes-ts: normalize Rest casts from Reflect.fields
description: Discovered from A downstream config loader. Using Haxe std Reflect.fields can generate Register.unsafeCast<Rest<any>>(...) in Reflect.ts without importing Rest, breaking TS strict typecheck. Normalize Rest cast targets to array types or otherwise avoid leaking unresolved Rest in expression casts.
acceptance_criteria: A genes-ts fixture using Reflect.fields typechecks; generated Reflect.ts does not contain unsafeCast<Rest<...>> or unresolved Rest; genes-ts tests pass.
close_reason: accepted: Reflect.fields fixture passes; TypeEmitter normalizes haxe.extern.Rest<T> TType aliases to T[]; full test guard rejects unsafeCast<Rest<...>> leaks; gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts:full, reran yarn test:genes-ts && yarn test:genes-ts:full after guard.

title: Prove imported @:native binding identity under local collisions
description: Reduce the remaining compatibility paths where a Haxe declaration combines @:native with @:jsRequire. The current compiler deliberately preserves the historical native-text accessor while ordinary imports now resolve from canonical origin identity. Prove dotted and non-dotted forms in both output profiles before deciding whether imported values must always use the collision-allocated local.
acceptance_criteria: Focused TS and classic fixtures cover dotted native/default, non-dotted native/named, a same-local collision, runtime markers, public type positions, source maps, and transaction-safe failure. The result either switches these forms to exact origin resolution or documents and diagnoses the narrower supported contract without Dynamic, untyped, casts, any, or broad unknown.
notes:
Claimed after the canonical binding-identity landing. Starting with fail-first dotted and non-dotted @:native + @:jsRequire collision fixtures across genes-ts, classic JS, public declarations, source maps, and runtime markers.
Observed standard Haxe 4.3.7: combined @:native + @:jsRequire always reads the package export. Fail-first Genes output already allocated NativeNamed__1 and NativeRoot__1 but constructor/type access bypassed them through raw native text. The focused fix now resolves package-backed declarations through canonical origins, while native-only Date and internal @:native typedef renaming remain direct. A built-in-name String runtime case is covered; Haxe core-type canonicalization in public positions is tracked separately.
Completed evidence: standard Haxe 4.3.7, genes-ts, and classic Genes now all treat @:jsRequire as the runtime value source when @:native is also present. Collision fixtures prove non-dotted named, dotted default/member, built-in-name runtime, native-only host global, public type, source-map, and TS 5/6/7 behavior. Focused gates and the complete yarn test:ci passed. The separate public-type ambiguity for a package extern whose native name is a Haxe core type remains intentionally open as genes-kxi.
close_reason: Package-backed native declarations now resolve through exact canonical import bindings in both output profiles, with standard-Haxe comparison, runtime/type/source-map evidence, and full CI green. Core-name public type modeling is separately tracked by genes-kxi.

title: Emit typed template literals without runtime assertions
description: Haxe interpolation lowers to string concatenation, which widens template-shaped values to string in generated TypeScript. Raw syntax preserves the target spelling but enters through Dynamic and forces Register.unsafeCast. Add a framework-neutral, target-polymorphic template-literal primitive so arbitrary libraries can retain template literal inference without runtime helpers or assertions.
design: Introduce an explicit generic template-literal marker rather than changing all Haxe string concatenation. A macro accepts an authored interpolated-string expression, reduces it to ordered literal chunks plus typed String expressions, and emits a compiler-recognized marker. Shared semantic validation owns arity and escaping; TypeScript prints a native template literal so contextual template-literal types remain precise, while classic JavaScript preserves equivalent ES behavior. The marker must disappear entirely from both outputs and must not introduce raw Dynamic, untyped, any, broad unknown, target assertions, or runtime imports in user modules.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- A typed Haxe helper emits a native TypeScript template literal without Register.unsafeCast or a runtime helper call.
- Static chunks and typed interpolations preserve evaluation order and evaluate each value once.
- Escaping covers backticks, backslashes, dollar-brace sequences, and multiline text.
- Classic Genes emits equivalent JavaScript and passes runtime evidence from the same Haxe source.
- Invalid non-literal templates fail with focused source diagnostics.
- The complete genes-ts and classic-JS CI gate passes.
notes: Implemented framework-neutral genes.TemplateLiteral.value with a typed extern marker, shared pre-output validation, native TypeScript template emission, and classic ordered concatenation. Added same-source positive and negative fixture coverage for exact template inference, ordinary interpolation widening, wrong-family rejection, arbitrary runtime String diagnostics, static literals, escaping, evaluation order, single evaluation, marker/assertion absence, runtime parity, and TypeScript 5.5/6/7 consumers. Verification passed: formatter check, git diff --check, yarn test:template-literals, yarn test:dual-output, yarn test:matrix:generated, and the complete yarn test:ci gate under the repository-required Node 20 and Haxe 4.3.7.
close_reason: Implemented and verified generalized typed template-literal emission in both Genes output profiles.

title: Classify Reflaxe.Elixir vendored Genes deltas
description: Build the three-way decision ledger from vanilla v0.4.14, downstream vendor commits, and current genes-ts. Separate observed behavior from inference and identify the smallest generic evidence for every delta.
acceptance_criteria: Async, literals, registry/bind, source maps, writer catch, whitespace, and packaging deltas each have one recorded disposition with source and executable-evidence references.
notes: Completed the three-way ledger at genes-ts 8989465, downstream snapshot 1ce84dc, and the peeled v0.4.14 source commit 9394b7e (the handoff's a5dccf2 is the annotated tag object).\nDispositions: modern typed async, registry globals, and iterator architecture supersede the vendor mechanisms; string emission and bind remain supported but under-tested; source-map record typing and inferred Writer catch are valid missing cleanups; assignment whitespace is formatting-only and requires a raw invariant; packaging is downstream-only.\nRecorded exact evidence, experiments, safe sequencing, and the downstream adoption boundary in docs/REFLAXE_ELIXIR_VENDOR_AUDIT.md. Verified the documentation-owned ts2hx gate with yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test:docs.
close_reason: Every substantive vendor delta now has an evidence-backed three-way disposition and an explicit follow-up owner.

title: Audit and absorb Reflaxe.Elixir vendored Genes improvements
description: Three-way audit of original Genes v0.4.14, Reflaxe.Elixir's vendored derivative, and current genes-ts. Classify every substantive delta, add missing generic regressions or fixes, preserve both output modes, and produce a downstream adoption handoff without changing the downstream vendor.
acceptance_criteria: Every substantive vendor delta has an evidence-backed disposition; valid generic improvements are independently tested and landed; superseded mechanisms have executable equivalence evidence; full Genes CI and disposable downstream verification pass before any adoption recommendation.
close_reason: Completed the three-way vendor audit, landed every justified generic correction with focused and full-CI evidence, proved superseded async/runtime behavior, and verified all five Reflaxe.Elixir clients plus bounded browser journeys in a disposable worktree. Downstream pinning and vendor removal remain separately owned.

title: Probe output symlink containment on supported hosts
description: OutputTransaction validates lexical containment but has no explicit symlink or reparse-point policy. Reproduce the exact pinned filesystem behavior before adding platform-specific hardening.
acceptance_criteria: A controlled casualty fixture proves whether target and stage symlinks can modify paths outside the output root. If reproducible, fail closed before touching the casualty; otherwise document the trusted-workspace boundary with executable evidence.
notes: Fail-first POSIX evidence on Haxe 4.3.7 showed that a generated package-directory symlink let a successful build write into an external casualty directory. The fix now checks each existing path component with Haxe's resolved full path before reading, writing, deleting, or cleaning compiler-owned transaction paths. Abort cleanup skips hostile links instead of following them. Target and stage casualties remain byte-identical. This is a preflight under the existing same-destination serialization contract, not an operating-system no-follow lock; Windows junction semantics remain unclaimed. The focused output-transaction and dual-output gates passed, followed by the complete pinned Node 20.19.3 yarn test:ci gate in 400.00 seconds.
close_reason: Reproduced the POSIX traversal and now fail closed before target or stage symlinks can touch an external path; focused gates and full CI pass.

title: Make generated public-surface auditing self-enrolling
description: The semantic TypeChecker audit is strong but callers hand-select include paths. A newly generated public module can evade the audit entirely.
acceptance_criteria: Every compiler-owned generated public module is audited or explicitly classified from an executable ownership inventory; a new inferred-any export cannot pass by path omission; exact boundary entries remain stale-detecting; type-export and profile gates pass.
notes:
Claimed after genes-qfn landed and its exact pushed revision passed full Genes CI and CodeQL. Starting with a fail-first inventory-enrollment fixture: an owned generated module with an inferred weak export must fail even when a caller omits it from a hand-maintained include list.
Implemented manifest-owned enrollment across every production public-surface audit. The gate now validates exact Genes v2 owner identities, canonical owned paths, TypeScript Program enrollment, and stale-detecting boundary or known-gap classifications. A fail-first policy fixture proves a caller-selected safe file can omit an owned inferred-any sibling, while inventory selection catches it. Per-module declaration ownership and a compile-local declaration guard bound recursively instantiated generic graphs. Existing full TypeScript and classic declaration weaknesses are classified exactly and owned by genes-ofy rather than presented as safe. Focused type/profile/example gates passed, followed by full pinned Node 20.19.3 yarn test:ci in 393.93 seconds, including both todoapp profiles and all ts2hx lanes.
Post-push release verification caught an intentional compatibility inventory drift that the pre-commit full gate could not see because the new policy fixtures were still untracked: strict-consumer-sources changed from 7 to 10. Reopened to update the reviewed metric, regenerate derived reports, rerun full CI, and verify the follow-up release.
Corrected the reviewed strict-consumer-sources inventory from 7 to 10 after the three new fixtures became tracked, regenerated both compatibility reports, and strengthened the evidence prose so exact classifications are not mistaken for proof of foreign-boundary soundness. The focused compatibility gate passed, followed by a fresh complete yarn test:ci in 415.34 seconds from the tracked repository state.
close_reason: Acceptance met and post-push evidence drift resolved: manifest-owned enrollment, exact classifications, reviewed compatibility inventory, focused gates, and a fresh full CI run are green.

title: Retire only manifest-owned stale ts2hx output in no-clean mode
description: Default no-clean translation copies the entire prior tree and never consumes the prior plannedFiles inventory, so renamed or deleted TypeScript sources survive indefinitely as stale Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: After source B is removed, a no-clean run removes only B's recognized prior planned output, preserves handwritten assets, rejects malformed ownership manifests, is deterministic, and passes the full ts2hx gate.
notes: Fail-first evidence: baseline face6ead successfully published A+B, then a no-clean A-only run retained ownership_test/Removed.hx despite removing it from the new plannedFiles inventory. The final correction validates the prior embedded schema-v3 envelope before staging, removes only stale plannedFiles inside the private stage, preserves colocated assets/handwritten Haxe, rejects malformed or escaping ownership without mutating prior output, and is deterministic. The focused test, complete ts2hx suite, and full yarn test:ci gate are green on Node 20.19.3.
close_reason: Baseline reproduced stale Removed.hx after a successful no-clean rerun. The schema-v3 ownership envelope is now validated before staging; only missing prior plannedFiles are removed inside the private stage, unowned files are preserved, malformed or escaping ownership fails without publication, identical reruns are deterministic, the focused test and full ts2hx suite pass, and yarn test:ci passed on Node 20.19.3 in 403.81s.

title: Coordinate ts2hx external diagnostics publication
description: The CLI writes diagnostics JSON after emitProjectToHaxe has committed the generated tree. A failing external write can return exit 2 after changing output.
acceptance_criteria: A valid translation with an invalid diagnostics target returns nonzero while leaving the prior output tree byte-identical and no stage/backup debris; documented process-failure and cross-filesystem guarantees are exact; strict diagnostics and full ts2hx gates pass.
close_reason: Reproduced the success-then-external-write failure, moved external manifest publication into the output rollback window, documented the process/crash boundary, and passed strict diagnostics, the full ts2hx suite, and yarn test:ci on Node 20.19.3.

title: Fail closed on malformed import-attribute metadata
description: Handwritten genes.importAttributeType metadata currently maps malformed arity, empty values, or nonliteral expressions to no attribute. Diagnose presence-with-invalid-shape before publication instead of silently dropping the loader contract.
acceptance_criteria: Nonliteral, wrong-arity, and empty metadata produce stable source-positioned diagnostics and preserve the prior tree; valid macro and handwritten attributes remain byte- and runtime-correct in both profiles.
close_reason: Malformed handwritten import attributes now fail during shared dependency planning with stable arity, literal, and empty-value diagnostics. The paired TypeScript/classic fixture preserves seeded prior output for every failure while the valid JSON loader path remains runtime-correct. Verified by yarn test:dual-output, yarn test:side-effect-import:evidence, yarn test:genes-ts:sourcemaps, compatibility report regeneration/check, and full yarn test:ci (369.10s).

title: Terminate module directive statements before following banner text
description: Codex review thread https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/genes-ts/pull/1#discussion_r3599692526 found an ASI correctness hole in the new directive prologue. emitString writes a bare literal plus newline, so a following genes.banner beginning with an expression-continuation token such as ( turns the directive into a call expression and throws before module execution.
design: Emit an explicit semicolon after every validated directive in the shared classic/TypeScript prologue emitter. Strengthen the module-directives fixture with a harmless banner that begins with ( so both runtimes exercise the ASI boundary. Update exact-output tests, source-map expectations where necessary, documentation, and the PR examples. Do not special-case banner contents or alter directive ordering/DCE behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Both implementation profiles emit each directive as a semicolon-terminated statement; an expression-continuation banner cannot attach to the preceding literal; TypeScript and classic runtime fixtures pass with the hostile banner; exact-output/docs/PR examples match; full yarn test:ci and replacement PR checks pass.
notes:
Implemented the review fix in both emitters through the shared directive-prologue path: every generated string directive now has an explicit semicolon. Strengthened the dual-profile fixture with an ASI-hostile banner beginning with ( and exact semicolon assertions. Local evidence: yarn test:module-directives passed; yarn test:ci passed in 323.27s; git diff --check clean. Awaiting commit, push, and replacement GitHub checks before closure.
Pushed corrective commit 2b5c336bec43ffc04c8184ffab3d72b19c6b0edf. PR body now documents exact safe and unsafe output plus the observed TypeError and discovery context. Replacement runs: genes-ts CI 29547759280 and CodeQL 29547759263; fast, security, classic, dependency-review, and advisory-preview jobs are green, with long Genes jobs/CodeQL still running. Review thread remains unresolved/non-outdated; no reply or resolution was posted without explicit authorization.
Final remote evidence for exact head 2b5c336bec43ffc04c8184ffab3d72b19c6b0edf: genes-ts CI run 29547759280 passed all 9 jobs; CodeQL run 29547759263 passed; gh pr checks reports all 11 check entries successful; PR is merge-clean. The review thread is intentionally left unresolved/non-outdated and without a reply because no authorization to mutate review-thread state was given.
close_reason: Fixed the actionable review finding by semicolon-terminating module directives in the shared emitter, added a dual-profile ASI-hostile runtime regression and exact documentation, pushed commit 2b5c336, and verified all replacement Genes CI and CodeQL checks green.

title: Make advisory CI checks fork-aware
description: PR #1 exposes two red checks unrelated to the compiler feature: GitHub dependency review is unsupported against fork repositories, and the deliberately non-blocking Haxe preview lane reports its known compatibility failure as a red check. Correct the workflow without weakening supported-toolchain, OSV, or secrets gates.
design: Keep the existing Dependency Review job and use github.event.repository.fork to select the native action or a documented fork fallback. Keep job-level Haxe preview continue-on-error for unexpected failures, add step-level tolerance only around preview compatibility suites, then report their outcomes in warnings and the job summary. Do not change compiler code, dependencies, or supported Haxe 4.3.7 gates.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency Review runs the native action on non-forks and completes with an explicit OSV-backed notice on forks; known Haxe preview test incompatibilities surface as annotations and step summaries without a red check; supported CI gates remain unchanged; workflow syntax and replacement PR checks are verified.
notes: Implemented and pushed bf409a5. Dependency Review now runs the native action on non-forks and emits an explicit OSV-backed notice on forks; Haxe preview retains job-level infrastructure protection while only its two known compatibility suites are advisory with visible annotations and summary outcomes. Local verification passed with actionlint 1.7.12, YAML parsing, bash -n for every embedded run block, simulated fork/advisory scripts, gitleaks, and OSV. Exact-head remote evidence: commit 917393684681d2d0fdbdd2c10ac41f81754eb343, genes-ts CI run 29546030314 passed all 9 jobs (including Dependency Review, Haxe preview, OSV, gitleaks, supported TypeScript/E2E and classic lanes), and CodeQL run 29546030312 passed. PR: https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/genes-ts/pull/1.
close_reason: Fork-aware dependency review and explicit Haxe-preview advisory reporting are implemented, locally validated, pushed, and green on the final PR head without weakening supported gates.

title: Add deterministic generic module directive prologues
description: Add a framework-neutral, literal-only way for Haxe modules to request ECMAScript directive prologues. Directives must be planned from typed metadata, deduplicated deterministically, and emitted before every import in both TypeScript-source and classic ESM profiles without changing DCE or embedding downstream directive names.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Multiple literal directives preserve deterministic source order and deduplicate exact repeats.
- TypeScript output places directives before all imports.
- Classic ESM output places the same valid directive prologue before all imports and runs successfully.
- Invalid arity, non-literal values, empty values, and conflicting module declarations fail with stable focused diagnostics before publication.
- Generic fixtures contain no downstream framework names or paths.

title: Classify pinned downstream failures and enforce Node runtime
description: The curated downstream runner always reports any nonzero command as downstream-failure-unclassified, even when the pinned manifest records an exact known downstream failure. It also begins mutating/building a checkout under an arbitrary host Node version, allowing native-addon ABI mismatches to masquerade as product smoke failures. Reproduced on pinned OpenCodeHX: the expected optional stream discriminant TS2345 errors appeared exactly; subsequent Node 23 smoke failed against a Node 20 better-sqlite3 binary, while the same smoke passed completely on installed Node 20.
design: Extend the manifest with machine-verifiable expected-failure evidence tied to one command. Capture and replay command output, classify only exact pinned matches as expected downstream failures, continue independent later stages, reject baseline drift/unexpected diagnostics, and fail preflight before checkout mutation unless the runner uses config/toolchains.json node.stable. Add focused runner policy tests and didactic contract documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Pinned OpenCodeHX's two exact TS2345 diagnostics classify as a known downstream failure and do not suppress typed-boundary/unit/smoke stages; changed/missing/extra failures remain unclassified and exit nonzero; a mismatched Node major is rejected before any downstream command runs with actionable guidance; passing profiles remain passing; no-execution contract validation and full Genes CI pass.
close_reason: Landed fail-closed downstream known-failure matching, stable-Node preflight, bounded streamed evidence capture, continuation of independent stages, deterministic report updates, and 15 focused policy contracts. Verified pinned PiMonoHX all-pass and pinned OpenCodeHX exact downstream TS2345 exception with later policy/unit/full smoke passing; full yarn test:ci passed in 282.44s.

title: Keep @:native global secondary externs independent from sibling @:jsRequire types
description: The pinned OpenCodeHX pressure test reduced a generic import-planning bug. A Haxe module can declare a secondary @:native("RegExp") global extern and, later in the same module, an unrelated @:jsRequire("ai") extern. Constructing the native secondary extern currently emits import * as RegExp from "ai" and new RegExp(...), so strict TypeScript reports that the module namespace is not constructable. @:native global identity must not inherit a sibling type's package dependency merely because both declarations share one Haxe module. The downstream nullable-string diagnostics observed in the same run are separate downstream type-model debt and are not part of this compiler issue.
design: Reduce to a package-neutral fixture containing one @:native global constructor and one unrelated @:jsRequire extern in the same Haxe module. Fix symbol/dependency planning generically; do not add knowledge of ai, OpenCodeHX, or RegExp-specific behavior. Cover both TS source and classic JS so the global remains a global in both profiles.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal generic fixture reproduces the mixed @:native/@:jsRequire module; generated TS uses the intended global constructor with no package import for it; the unrelated @:jsRequire value still imports correctly; classic JS executes the same global constructor behavior; strict TS, dual-output, package-shape, and full CI gates pass.
close_reason: Fixed shared dependency ownership so explicit @:native secondary externs remain host globals while metadata-free secondary externs retain primary @:jsRequire ownership; four-oracle dual-output, package-shape, pinned OpenCodeHX, and full CI gates pass.

title: Emit shared WebIDL gap globals in classic declarations
description: Classic Genes .d.ts output can include Haxe 4.3.7 DOM extern declarations that reference PositionError and FetchObserver, but TypeScript lib.dom does not provide those historical WebIDL names. TS-source mode already emits narrow ambient support in StdTypes.ts; classic StdTypes.d.ts must consume the same semantic contract so a complete generated declaration tree type-checks with skipLibCheck:false.
design: Extract the host-gap declarations from the TS-specific printer into a small shared StdTypes support emitter. Keep TS-only runtime metadata augmentations in the TS emitter. Append the shared declare-global block to classic StdTypes.d.ts and add a generic strict declaration-tree regression; do not add todoapp shims or weaken skipLibCheck.
acceptance_criteria: Classic StdTypes.d.ts declares precise PositionError and FetchObserver globals from the shared support model; the TS StdTypes output remains unchanged; the complete classic declaration tree type-checks with skipLibCheck:false; focused classic declaration and dual-profile example gates pass.
close_reason: Extracted the Haxe 4.3.7 WebIDL gaps into shared TS/classic StdTypes support and added a generic strict classic declaration regression on TS 5.5/6/7.

title: Prove every example under TS and classic JS profiles
description: Make checked-in examples enforce the product contract that one ordinary Haxe source tree can emit strict TypeScript/TSX or modern classic Genes ESM JavaScript. The todoapp must gain explicit classic web/server profiles without source forks; genes.ts helpers and metadata must erase or lower gracefully. The smaller typescript-target example must also be covered by aggregate example QA.
design: Keep target policy in build profiles and shared compiler helpers, never downstream-specific compiler branches. Build TS profiles and classic profiles into isolated trees. Exercise strict TS/declaration consumers where applicable, bundle or execute both runtimes, compare stable API/UI behavior, and make a manifest enumerate every checked-in example/profile owner.
acceptance_criteria: All directories immediately under examples/ are listed in one checked-in QA manifest and exercised by an aggregate script; todoapp web and server build from examples/todoapp/src in both genes.ts and classic modes; classic web bundle and classic server API/Playwright smoke pass without Haxe source forks; target-polymorphic helpers/metadata degrade cleanly; deterministic snapshots or bounded shape checks cover classic output; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Every checked-in example now owns TS and classic profiles; the minimal program compares exact runtime transcripts and the same-source React/Express todoapp passes strict builds, API smoke, and identical Playwright journeys in both modes. Full yarn test:ci is green.

title: Extract shared PublicSurface model
description: Replace target-local public member collection with an immutable PublicSurface model for exported classes, interfaces, typedefs, inheritance, overload identity, visibility, and type-parameter substitution. Both TS implementation interfaces and classic declaration emission consume it.
design: Build on the landed pre-DCE closed-interface fix. First compare the model against existing TS and d.ts surfaces, retain runtime-marker emission as a separate concern, and migrate consumers without reopening interfaces or coupling collection to printing.
acceptance_criteria: TS interfaces and classic declarations consume the same checked public member facts; inherited/generic/overloaded members have focused fixtures; implementation-private and runtime-only fields do not leak; current negative consumers and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Landed the shared pre-DCE PublicSurface model and migrated TypeScript interfaces plus classic declarations to it, including inherited generic members, overload identity, visibility boundaries, and paired negative/runtime fixtures. Full yarn test:ci passes.

title: Add exact source maps determinism and output budgets
description: Upgrade source-map and output-quality evidence with exact token mappings, mapped thrown stacks, clean-build tree hashes, and per-fixture byte/token/temp/module/import metrics.
design: Compare semantics rather than byte identity with vanilla. Begin performance as report-only until noise is calibrated. Require reviewed manifest entries for legitimate size/temp/module growth.
acceptance_criteria: Two clean builds hash identically after documented normalization; representative TS/classic tokens and stack frames map to exact Haxe locations; unexplained module growth or no-temp fixture regressions fail; budgets are checked in and deterministic.
close_reason: Landed bounded exact output-quality evidence for the authoritative dual corpus. Added six exact TS/classic Haxe token mappings, a retained native-Error stack probe proving direct classic JS-to-Haxe and separate JS-to-TS plus TS-to-Haxe stages, two-clean-build normalized tree hashes, source-map-aware lowering-temp classification, exact module/temp/import baselines, 5% byte/token ceilings, zero-temp fixtures, reviewed manifest metadata, and report-only Haxe build timings. Integrated the gate into dual-output, generated matrix, acceptance, docs, and profile ownership. Verified yarn test:output-quality, yarn test:dual-output, yarn test:genes-ts:sourcemaps, yarn test:vulns, and full yarn test:ci in 192.24s (306 classic assertions, TS5/6/7, exact quality hashes, Playwright 3/3, ts2hx 53 snapshots + 3 roundtrips + strict diagnostics).

title: Introduce target-neutral JSX intent and capability policy
description: Represent tags/components/props/children as target-neutral JSX intent before printing and make TSX, TypeScript createElement, and classic JS capabilities explicit.
design: Preserve inline markup as default-on in genes.ts mode and marker compatibility during migration. Classic mode must either lower through a configured runtime or fail before output with a source-positioned capability diagnostic.
acceptance_criteria: Existing TSX profiles stay green; invalid intrinsic/component prop and child fixtures are rejected; same-source classic JSX either executes equivalent intent or receives a stable pre-output diagnostic; docs match behavior; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Implemented shared target-neutral JsxPlan and capability policy consumed by TypeScript and classic JS emitters; moved JSX runtime imports into DependencyPlan; added fail-closed source-positioned diagnostics, exact-once lifted-value semantics, TS negative cases, and identical-source TSX/classic runtime fixtures. Updated contracts/docs and deterministic todoapp snapshots. Verified with yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:dual-output, and full yarn test:ci (306 classic assertions, TS 5.5/6/7, Playwright 3/3, ts2hx 53 snapshots + 3 roundtrips + strict diagnostics) in 192.03s.

title: Centralize TS5 TS6 TS7 and Haxe toolchain lanes
description: Centralize compiler versions and distinguish generated-code compatibility from programmatic TypeScript API compatibility. Keep Haxe 4.3.7 blocking, add TS6 API and TS7 generated-output lanes, and track Haxe 5 preview nonblocking.
design: Use one checked-in version manifest consumed by scripts and CI. Do not pretend TS7 exposes the historical Program API; isolate ts2hx/dts2hx API use behind an adapter. Remove obsolete config assumptions through fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: No repeated hard-coded TypeScript version literals in test runners; required generated projects check under the documented TS lanes; TS API tools use the supported bridge lane; Haxe preview is visible but nonblocking; version docs match CI.
close_reason: Centralized Node/Haxe/TypeScript versions in config/toolchains.json; added TS5 generated-output floor, TS6 Program/TypeChecker bridge and output lane, TS7 output-only lane, blocking stable Haxe/Node lanes, and visible nonblocking Haxe 5 preview CI. Replaced runner literals with manifest-backed commands, isolated compiler API imports behind adapters, removed obsolete baseUrl/node10 assumptions, added cross-version ArrayBuffer and semantic-audit compatibility fixes, documented lane ownership, validated workflows with actionlint, and passed full yarn test:ci in 216.38s.

title: Extract runtime type and declaration DependencyPlan
description: Replace no-op type printing and conservative reachability workarounds with an immutable dependency plan whose edges distinguish runtime values, runtime side effects, type-only symbols, and declaration-only symbols.
design: Retain the existing Dependencies import-form machinery. First run graph extraction in shadow/compare mode, add provenance and unresolved diagnostics, then switch emitters after output and DCE invariants are proven.
acceptance_criteria: No-op printer discovery and silent Context.getType catch are removed; DCE/type-only fixtures pass; every referenced symbol has a validated edge/global; runtime imports/module counts stay within reviewed baselines; yarn test:ci passes.
notes:
PublicSurface integration established a deliberate temporary boundary: classic class declarations intersect the pre-DCE public surface with post-DCE runtime fields, while interfaces remain complete. Removing that intersection requires declaration-only edges plus explicit abstract-instance surface modeling; blindly emitting every KAbstractImpl field can expose invalid static owner generics (for example an instance Future<T>.status represented on the static implementation class). The final plan must retain referenced declaration types without broadening classic JS DCE.
Landing correction: declaration-only reachability is now independent, but the default classic class declaration intentionally remains intersected with post-DCE runtime members. Removing that boundary would make .d.ts promise missing JavaScript. Complete class-library surfaces now belong to the separate opt-in reusable-library profile created from this issue. Shadow comparison matched the full TS and classic import trees; full yarn test:ci passed in 191.81s.
close_reason: Landed immutable runtime/type/declaration DependencyPlan edges with typed refs and provenance; removed sink-printer discovery and silent Context.getType recovery; preserved import alias parity via ordered edges; separated implementation and declaration expansion so classic DCE stays compact; added TS type-only and classic declaration-only/no-orphan-map fixtures; shadow checks, focused gates, snapshots, and full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Extract shared NullishContract semantics
description: Represent Haxe Null<T>, JavaScript undefined, optional properties, Undefinable<T>, native map absence, and iterator completion as distinct shared semantic facts consumed by TS, classic JS, and declaration emission.
design: Introduce a narrow semantic classifier before expanding any target IR. Target printers choose syntax, but may not silently redefine absence semantics. Add paired TS/classic fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: A checked nullish matrix distinguishes missing, undefined, null, optional, and T under strict TS flags and classic runtime traces; no scattered Map-specific target check remains as the sole model; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Landed shared NullishContract across TS, classic JS decisions, and declaration emission with exact strict consumers, paired runtime traces, precedence-safe projections, docs, and green full CI.

title: Add semantic exported-surface and negative type gates
description: Go beyond lexical any/unknown scanning by auditing generated exported TypeScript and classic declaration surfaces with the TypeScript Compiler API, AST index-signature policy, strict external consumers, and negative @ts-expect-error fixtures.
design: Keep the cheap scanner, but treat it only as one layer. Add provenance/owner/reason for allowed runtime boundaries. Build on the closed-interface and classic-nullability P0 fixes without reopening types.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler-API export audit detects explicit and inferred public any/unknown; ordinary index signatures are rejected unless explicitly modeled; negative consumers fail if invalid member/nullish/JSX operations become legal; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Landed TypeScript-Compiler-API exported-surface auditing, exact provenance boundary manifests, unsafe/inferred/imported type and index-signature fixtures, TS/classic IMap enforcement, JSX negative-consumer guards, and a blocking CI script. Full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Architecture and product-readiness roadmap
description: Implement the evidence-backed post-audit roadmap for genes-ts as a shared Haxe-to-TypeScript and classic Haxe-to-JavaScript compiler. Preserve classic runtime behavior, strengthen TypeScript/public declarations, and borrow only narrow architecture/testing concepts from sibling compilers.
design: Proceed incrementally through shared semantic facts and lightweight normalized nodes: PublicSurface, NullishContract, DependencyPlan, TempPlan/NamePlan, and JsxIntent. Do not port to Reflaxe or build separate TS/classic semantic engines. Each child lands with generic fixtures and full yarn test:ci.
acceptance_criteria: All P1 child gates pass; both output modes remain first-class; remaining P2 work is represented by explicit Beads and generated evidence never overstates scope.
close_reason: Completed all 14 evidence-backed audit roadmap children: shared semantic seams, TS/classic dual-output compatibility, strict type/declaration gates, toolchain and package-shape matrices, ts2hx fail-closed semantic evidence, examples/todoapp parity, and generated compatibility reporting are green under full CI.

title: ts2hx React type-only imports must emit compileable typed Haxe
description: A generic TSX fixture with type-only imports from react currently reports success but emits unresolved ReactElement/MouseEvent/HTMLAnchorElement names plus a generated React extern containing Dynamic. It also maps TS optional/undefined JSX props to null, weakening React prop semantics. Reduce the constructs into a reusable fixture and make ts2hx output compileable, typed Haxe without Dynamic/untyped or downstream-specific rules.
acceptance_criteria: A generic React TSX fixture exercises type-only ReactElement and generic MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>, dynamic intrinsic tags, optional JSX attributes/handlers, fragments, map callbacks, and nullable children; generated Haxe has no Dynamic/untyped/unsupported placeholders, compiles through genes-ts, re-emits strict TSX, and focused plus full genes CI pass.
close_reason: Both callable-guard operand orders are generically covered and full CI passes

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Implemented generic React/TSX migration support: typed ReactElement/MouseEvent type-only imports, DOM element mapping, TS optional-field preservation, undefined JSX values, JS truthiness and callable guards, indexed Array callbacks, dynamic intrinsic React tags, nullable JSX child typing, and lifted JSX marker prop recovery in genes-ts. Added react-types fixture with strong-Haxe guard, Haxe compile smoke, strict genes-ts TSX roundtrip, and no-any/unknown user-module guard. Passed focused yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test and full yarn test:ci (208.18s, including classic, genes-ts acceptance, 3 todoapp browser E2E tests, security, versions, and ts2hx).

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa

WPHX-404's strong-Haxe gate passes generated-Haxe compile, strict TSX roundtrip, and zero weak user-module markers. Full yarn test:ci passed in 122.21s, including security/version checks, 288 classic assertions, acceptance, 3 Playwright E2E tests, 48 ts2hx snapshot files, and 3 roundtrip fixtures.

title: ts2hx: preserve module-local bindings and regex replacements
description: WPHX-403's real TypeScript leaf-package roundtrip reduced three generic ts2hx gaps: calls to non-exported module functions are emitted without a Haxe-visible owner, references to module-level RegExp constants are not lowered as value references, and String.replace with RegExp/string arguments falls back to an unsupported throw. Add minimal compiler fixtures and generic lowering so these ordinary TypeScript constructs roundtrip through Haxe and genes-ts without downstream names or patches.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal generic fixtures cover private module helper calls, module-level RegExp constants, and RegExp string replacement; generated Haxe compiles and runs through the roundtrip harness; full yarn test:ci passes.
notes: Reduced from WPHX-403. Generic fix preserves module-private declarations, maps TypeScript RegExp to typed Haxe EReg, and lowers regex test/replace via checker-aware EReg calls. Added module-regexp snapshot and full TS→Haxe→genes-ts→strict TS runtime roundtrip. yarn test:ci passed on 2026-07-09/10.
close_reason: Generic module-local binding and RegExp roundtrip support implemented with minimized fixture; full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Fix high-severity sigstore vulnerability blocking full CI
description: The mandatory yarn test:ci gate fails in test:vulns because osv-scanner reports GHSA-52v5-jr5w-gjxr against sigstore@3.1.0. Resolve the dependency generically in genes-ts without downstream-specific changes, preserve lockfile determinism, and rerun the complete compiler CI gate. Discovered by WordPressHX WPHX-401 at commit 85d26cf4e7b853297a99aa26cf3651e187f45b7a.
acceptance_criteria: yarn test:vulns reports no unallowlisted high/critical vulnerabilities; yarn test:ci passes classic Genes JS, genes-ts TypeScript, acceptance, snapshots/runtime, security, version, and ts2hx gates; package and lockfile changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Resolved GHSA-52v5-jr5w-gjxr by forcing sigstore 4.1.1 in the generic dependency resolution and refreshing yarn.lock. yarn test:vulns reports no issues and the full yarn test:ci gate passes secrets, vulnerabilities, versions, classic Genes JS runtime tests, genes-ts TypeScript acceptance/snapshots/runtime, todoapp Playwright, and ts2hx snapshots/roundtrips.

title: genes-ts: metadata-gate private helper lowering
description: OpenCodeHX build coverage showed global private static lowering is still too broad for existing downstream code. Keep the generic lowering implementation, but require an explicit metadata marker on private static helpers so projects can clean declaration surfaces at known helper boundaries without changing unrelated class methods.
acceptance_criteria:
- Private static helper lowering requires an explicit metadata marker such as @:genesLowerPrivateHelper
- The basic genes-ts fixture proves annotated sync and async static helpers lower out of declarations
- The no-js-es polish fixture no longer opts into helper lowering
- yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:ci, and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass
close_reason: accepted: private static helper lowering now requires explicit @:genesLowerPrivateHelper metadata; basic fixture covers sync and async annotated helpers; no-js-es no longer opts in; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:ci, and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass

title: genes-ts: scope private helper lowering to static methods
description: OpenCodeHX build coverage showed the new private helper lowering is too broad for instance methods: lowered private instance/accessor/generic helpers can lose prototype availability, class type parameters, or method-value paths. Scope the feature to private static source helpers for the original d.ts leak, preserve async helper emission, and leave instance lowering for a later dedicated fixture.
acceptance_criteria:
- -D genes.ts.lower_private_helpers lowers private static source helper methods out of class declarations
- Private instance methods remain class methods under the define
- Async private static helpers emit async module helper functions
- yarn test:genes-ts and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass
close_reason: accepted: scoped private helper lowering to private static source methods, preserved async helper emission, kept private instance methods class-shaped, and proved with yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci

title: genes-ts: hide private helper methods from generated declarations
description: Private Haxe helper methods in user classes are emitted as public TypeScript class methods in genes-ts source output. When downstream projects run tsc declaration emit, those helpers become part of the public .d.ts surface even though they are implementation details. Reduce this as a generic genes-ts class emission issue, not a downstream-specific case.
acceptance_criteria:
- Private source-owned Haxe helper methods used by public methods do not appear by name in tsc-emitted .d.ts files
- Generated TS still typechecks and runs when helpers are called directly or passed as function values
- Haxe stdlib/js support helper methods keep their existing runtime-accessible shape
- yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci pass
close_reason: accepted: private source helper methods can lower to unexported module helpers behind -D genes.ts.lower_private_helpers; fixture checks prove helper names stay out of tsc declarations; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci pass

title: Add generic genes.ts JSON value and codec helpers
description: Downstream OpenCodeHX exposed the need for a reusable target-polymorphic native JSON value layer: recursive TypeScript JsonValue/JsonObject/JsonArray aliases, checked Json.value/object/array construction for Haxe-owned literals/records, JsonCodec.parse/stringify/narrow runtime validation for Unknown boundaries, and paired genes-ts/classic Genes fixtures. This must be generic compiler/library work with no OpenCodeHX product knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: genes.ts exposes generic JSON value/object/array primitives with recursive TS aliases; Haxe-owned JSON literals/records compile without Unknown.fromBoundary, any, or call-site casts; Unknown boundaries narrow through runtime JsonCodec guards; negative fixtures reject non-JSON values; classic Genes output preserves native JS runtime shapes; full genes CI gate passes.
close_reason: accepted: added generic genes.ts JsonValue/JsonObject/JsonArray/JsonPrimitive/JsonNonNullValue primitives, checked Json.value/object/array constructors, JsonCodec parse/narrow/stringify with documented unsafeAssumeJson containment, recursive TS alias emission including downstream abstracts over JsonValue, array/rest union-parenthesis emission, runtime JsonValue tests, and json-abstract snapshot fixture; also resolved the existing @sigstore/core OSV lockfile gate with a resolution so full CI is clean. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts:minimal, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test, yarn test:vulns, yarn test:versions, SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance, yarn test:ts2hx, yarn test:ci, git diff --check.

title: Fix generic @:async method type parameters
description: @:async build macro failed to resolve method-local type parameters in Promise<T> return annotations, causing generic async helpers to fail with Type not found : T. Fix must preserve the declared generic signature while keeping Promise-shape inspection typed and local.
acceptance_criteria: Aggregate CI result is recorded, with unrelated blockers tracked separately.
notes: Implemented macro-only local type-parameter erasure for Promise shape inspection so @:async methods can keep Promise<T> return signatures. Added TestAsyncAwait.genericIdentity<T> regression fixture. Validation run: yarn test, yarn test:genes-ts:minimal, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots (rerun standalone after parallel artifact race), yarn test:genes-ts:sourcemaps, yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, yarn test:versions, yarn test:ts2hx, SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance. yarn test:ci is blocked by pre-existing OSV @sigstore/core finding tracked as genes-2wl.
close_reason: Fixed generic @:async Promise<T> method handling with macro-only type-param erasure and regression coverage; focused gates pass. Full yarn test:ci remains blocked by unrelated OSV issue genes-2wl.

title: Resolve OSV finding for @sigstore/core
description: Full yarn test:ci currently stops at yarn test:vulns because osv-scanner reports GHSA-jfc7-64v2-mr8c for @sigstore/core 2.0.0 in yarn.lock, fixed in 3.2.1. This appears unrelated to compiler async changes but blocks the mandatory aggregate CI script.
acceptance_criteria: yarn test:vulns passes without suppressing the finding, or the vulnerability is documented/allowlisted with an explicit maintainer-approved reason.
close_reason: Verified yarn test:vulns reports no issues with the resolved lockfile; full yarn test:ci passed.

title: genes-ts: add unknown narrowing primitives
description: Add reusable genes.ts primitives for Unknown narrowing, read-only unknown records, and read-only unknown arrays. The helpers must stay generic, work in TypeScript output and classic JS output, and keep domain/schema decoding outside the compiler.
close_reason: accepted: added generic UnknownNarrow/UnknownRecord/UnknownArray helpers, fixed raw undefined preservation in genes-ts placeholders, covered with snapshots, strict generated TS, classic runtime tests, and full CI.

title: genes-ts: parenthesize nullish operands in null comparisons
description: OpenCodeHX exposed generated TypeScript like options.metadata ?? null != null, which TypeScript parses as options.metadata ?? (null != null). genes-ts should preserve Haxe comparison semantics by parenthesizing nullish-coalescing operands when they are emitted inside == null or != null comparisons.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a generic genes-ts snapshot fixture for a nullish-coalescing operand compared with null
- Generated TypeScript emits (expr ?? null) != null rather than expr ?? null != null
- yarn test:ci passes
close_reason: accepted: fixed generic genes-ts null comparison emission for nullish-coalescing operands; added BoundaryTypes snapshot coverage; yarn test:ci passed

title: genes-ts: parenthesize raw syntax templates before chained access
description: A downstream Haxe expression of the generic shape value.orNull()[0].field exposed malformed TypeScript from genes.ts.Undefinable.orNull(), which lowers through js.Syntax.code('{0} ?? null', value). When that raw nullish-coalescing template is used as the receiver of array/field access, generated TS can become '(value ?? null) ?? null[0].field' instead of parenthesizing the raw expression before the chained access. This is a generic expression-precedence/codegen bug, not downstream-specific.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal genes-ts fixture using genes.ts.Undefinable<Array<T>>.orNull()[0].field or an equivalent js.Syntax.code nullish-coalescing receiver; generated TypeScript parenthesizes correctly and strict tsc passes; existing raw template/native-field behavior stays intact; yarn test:genes-ts, snapshots/full as relevant, classic tests, and full yarn test:ci pass before downstream work resumes.
close_reason: accepted: fixed raw js.Syntax.code placeholder-template receivers before chained access; added Undefinable<Array<T>>.orNull()[0].field snapshot fixture; verified yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn build:example:todoapp, git diff --check, and full yarn test:ci

title: genes-ts: import promise thenable casts in full output
description: Full Genes CI after restoring the vulnerability gate reaches test:acceptance but tsc fails in tests/genes-ts/full/out/src-gen/tests/TestAsyncAwait.ts: generated code emits Register.unsafeCast<ThenableStruct<any>>(null) without importing or declaring ThenableStruct in that module. The alias exists in generated js/lib/Promise.ts, so type dependency/import planning needs to cover this generic special type when it appears in emitted casts.
acceptance_criteria: Full Genes CI passes. A focused fixture or existing full fixture proves emitted ThenableStruct casts either import the alias, qualify it, or avoid the unresolved type; no downstream-specific codegen special cases are added.
close_reason: accepted: existing TestAsyncAwait full fixture now emits await Promise.resolve(null) without ThenableStruct<any>; yarn test:genes-ts:full and full yarn test:ci passed

title: genes-ts: preserve call argument context inside raw syntax placeholders
description: A downstream async HTTP client reduction exposed that @:await lowers through js.Syntax.code("await {0}", call), and raw syntax placeholder emission currently delegates the placeholder expression to the base JS value emitter. That bypasses genes-ts call-argument expected-type context, so optional anonymous fields passed to Null<T> parameters emit T | null | undefined inside awaited calls. Fix this generically while preserving raw template behavior and snapshot it with an async/await fixture.
close_reason: accepted: raw js.Syntax.code placeholders now emit placeholder expressions through the TS value emitter, preserving call-argument expected-type context inside @:await. Added AsyncFoo metadata-await fixture where an optional field is passed to a Null<String> parameter inside an awaited Promise call. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts; UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test; git diff --check. Full yarn test:ci remains blocked by pre-existing OSV dependency findings tracked as genes-6f5.

title: genes-ts: preserve TS field emission when normalizing optional reads
description: Follow-up from optional-field nullable-param work. The new emitValue optional-read normalization used the base JS emitter for the inner field, so nested reads like record.server.port emitted (record.server.port ?? null) and skipped the TS emitter's nullable-receiver assertion. Fix the helper generically so optional-read normalization reuses TS field emission and preserves strict-null output for nested optional fields.
close_reason: accepted: optional-field normalization now re-enters TS expression emission and suppresses nested optional-field null normalization only while emitting a receiver that is immediately guarded by a TS non-null assertion. Added nested optional-field fixture and snapshot. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts; UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test; git diff --check.

title: genes-ts: normalize optional fields when passed to Null parameters
description: Discovered from a downstream Copilot HTTP client slice. Haxe optional anonymous fields can be absent at runtime, and Haxe treats absent reads as null-like, but generated strict TypeScript passes T | null | undefined into helpers typed as Null<T> (T | null), causing TS2345. Reduce to a generic fixture where an optional field is passed directly to a Null<T> parameter and fix genes-ts without downstream-specific code.

title: Model public types for package externs whose native name matches a Haxe core type
description: The imported @:native identity experiment proved that a package-backed class annotated @:native("String") correctly uses the package constructor after exact accessor resolution. Haxe 4.3.7 also canonicalizes that declaration as core String in public type reachability, so forcing a distinct imported public type caused the classic declaration projection to lack an honest origin mapping. Reduce built-in-name cases such as String and RegExp across Haxe 4.3.7 and the Haxe 5 preview before choosing a typed wrapper, diagnostic, or compiler projection.
acceptance_criteria: Focused fixtures distinguish runtime value identity from public type identity for package-backed externs whose native names collide with Haxe core or JS built-in types. TS/classic implementations and classic declarations either preserve a precise imported type or emit a source-positioned supported-boundary diagnostic; no Dynamic, untyped, cast, any, or broad unknown workaround is introduced.
notes:
Starting with fail-first fixtures for package-backed externs whose runtime native names overlap Haxe core or JavaScript built-ins. The experiment will compare value emission, public type reachability, declarations, and typed macro facts under Haxe 4.3.7 and 5.0.0-preview.1 before changing compiler behavior.
Observed fail-first evidence:
- Haxe 4.3.7 onAfterTyping still exposed package_shapes.native_string.NativeString as its exact TInst, with native=String and jsRequire=true. Later JavaScript preparation changed how the generator classified that type.
- genes-ts emitted a package constructor call but primitive string as the public type, producing TS2322 and TS2339. Classic declarations likewise lost the imported instance type.
- Adding the existing @:ts.instanceType contract fixed TS spelling, but classic declaration planning initially failed with GENES-IMPORT-BINDING-MISSING-001 because TypeReferenceCollector discarded the declaration through its built-in String/RegExp branches before import reachability.

Implemented correction:
- TypeReferenceCollector now honors the explicit imported-instance contract before built-in spelling heuristics, validates it once, and retains the exact declaration for type/declaration reachability. Printers remain syntax-only.
- Package-backed native String or RegExp values used in emitted type positions without @:ts.instanceType now fail before publication with source-positioned GENES-EXTERN-BUILTIN-NAME-TYPE-001. Runtime-only package use remains supported.
- No Dynamic, untyped, cast, Any, generated any/unknown, Reflect, or printer-local workaround was added.

Executable evidence:
- yarn test:binding-identity passed on Haxe 4.3.7 and Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1, covering String and RegExp values, public TS source, tsc declarations, classic declarations, runtime transcripts, source maps, TS 5/6/7, and four fail-closed rollback cases.
- test:interop:module-shapes, test:classic:dts, test:genes-ts:snapshots, test:output-quality, and test:output-transaction passed. Stable output hashes remained TS 77:e90d4bec0220 and classic 120:335fc77d43bd.
- Full Node 20 yarn test:ci passed in 531.47 seconds, including both output profiles, examples/Playwright, compatibility and security owners, and ts2hx.
close_reason: Precise imported-instance reachability, source diagnostics, dual-profile fixtures, stable/preview Haxe evidence, TS 5/6/7, maps, rollback, and full CI are green.

title: Lower hxnodejs legacy __js__ globals under Haxe 5 preview
description: Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 types hxnodejs 10's inline js.Node.console getter as __js__("console"). The custom Genes emitter currently delegates that node to Haxe's generator, which leaves __js__ in generated TypeScript and causes TS2304. Reduce the behavior independently from import identity and preserve classic JavaScript plus supported Haxe 4.3.7 semantics.
acceptance_criteria: A focused hxnodejs fixture uses typed js.Node console/process access under Haxe 4.3.7 and the configured Haxe preview. genes-ts and classic output contain the intended native globals, never a raw __js__ identifier, and execute/type-check without Dynamic, untyped, cast, any, or broad unknown added by Genes. The compatibility boundary and any unavoidable legacy raw-syntax handling are documented beside the emitter.
notes:
Starting with a reduced typed hxnodejs fixture under both Haxe 4.3.7 and 5.0.0-preview.1. The first step is to capture Haxe typed-expression and standard-JS behavior for console/process before changing the Genes emitter; no raw string rewrite or weak target type will be assumed.

Fail-first evidence: the typed hxnodejs APIs js.Node.process and js.Node.console compiled under Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1, but both Genes profiles emitted raw __js__("process")/__js__("console") calls. TypeScript rejected those calls with TS2304, and standard Haxe 5 JavaScript output also left the same raw helper calls.

The shared expression emitter now recognizes only hxnodejs's narrow legacy shape: exactly one literal ASCII JavaScript identifier. It emits that validated global name directly in TS and classic JS. It does not accept arbitrary source text, and the change adds no Dynamic, untyped, cast, Any, broad unknown, Reflect, or raw post-processing.

Verification: the focused probe passes on Haxe 4.3.7 and Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1 in both output profiles. It checks runtime process/console access, TS 5/6/7, classic and generated declarations, exact source ownership for process.version and console.log, and absence of __js__. Stable snapshots and output-quality hashes remained unchanged (TS 77:e90d4bec0220; classic 120:335fc77d43bd). Full Node 20 yarn test:ci passed in 484.76s, including browser examples and ts2hx.
