title: Require concrete behavior examples in PRs
description: Make PR #1 and future behavior-changing Genes pull requests specific enough to review without reconstructing intent from the diff. Add concrete positive, pre-feature/unsupported, and invalid-input examples to PR #1, and persist a repository instruction requiring equivalent specificity in future PR descriptions.
design: Add a narrowly scoped Pull request descriptions rule to AGENTS.md. Update PR #1 through gh CLI with a Behavior by example section derived from the generic regression fixture. Keep examples consumer-neutral and distinguish unsupported old behavior, unsafe global-banner workaround, valid output, and validation failure.
acceptance_criteria: PR #1 shows minimal Haxe source, exact generated output, concrete behavior without the feature, and one invalid example with its stable diagnostic/rollback result; repository agent instructions require concrete before/after and positive/negative examples for behavior changes; instruction is generic and does not reference a downstream repository.
notes: PR #1 now includes a dedicated discovery section: NextJsHx App Router boundary work required use client/use server before generated imports; the safe adapter workaround exposed that Genes lacked a standard per-module prologue; genes.banner was global and unvalidated; the case was reduced to arbitrary literal, consumer-neutral metadata and alpha/beta fixtures. AGENTS.md now generally requires safe concrete discovery context, exact observed output/failure, why existing mechanisms are inadequate, and why the generalized fix belongs here while code/fixtures stay consumer-neutral. Added and pushed as 9173936.
close_reason: Concrete discovery rationale and general PR rule added and pushed

title: ts2hx: support bound package ESM imports
description: Preserve runtime-bound package imports such as default, named, aliased, mixed, and namespace imports. The current translator generates @:jsRequire extern access and intentionally fails these effective ESM requests. Scoping confirmed that the generated extern fields are Dynamic, so package loading, request order, binding coalescing, and strong imported-value types must all be proven before support can be claimed.
design: Follow the existing GPT-5.6 bound-request architecture instead of redesigning printers. First extend the semantic differential with a local network-free ESM package and exact emitted-import assertions. Test whether the shared external request carrier plus ordinary strongly typed extern references coalesces at the source request slot. Support only immutable imported package bindings whose TypeScript declarations have a mechanically strong Haxe representation and whose original TS, classic Genes, and genes-ts traces and import shapes agree. Keep weak or unsupported declaration shapes, mutable/live bindings, CommonJS lowering, attributes, runtime re-exports, and request-free standard Haxe fail-closed. If the existing dependency projection or TypeChecker-to-Haxe boundary cannot express the contract without new semantics, stop for focused architecture review rather than adding string rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Original TypeScript, classic Genes, and genes-ts execute the same default/named/alias/mixed/namespace package trace under -dce full; generated TS passes pinned TS 5/6/7 without any or unknown in user modules; imports retain source request order and execute the package once; unused effective requests remain retained; strict and assisted transactions preserve unsupported variants; the package-bound canonical failure is removed only after executable evidence and documentation counts are regenerated; yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test and full yarn test:ci pass.
notes: Implemented the first production bound-package ESM subset. Effective TypeScript requests now consume the checker-owned package extern plan and emit strong @:jsRequire members for declaration-file primitive consts plus single-signature non-generic functions with required primitive parameters and primitive/Void results. Default, named, alias, mixed, static namespace, duplicate, and unused verbatim requests are covered; duplicate requests coalesce to one final import and one package initialization. Broader declarations, mutable/live values, transform-synthesized uses, attributes, computed/identity namespace use, sanitized export names, and colliding extern module names retain TS2HX-MODULES-ESM-RUNTIME-PACKAGE-BOUND-001. A local package differential matches original TypeScript, classic Genes, and genes-ts, and generated TS passes TS 5.5/6/7. Strict and assisted collision failures preserve the prior tree. Documentation, compatibility evidence, snapshots, and AGENTS guidance were updated. Verification: yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test passed in 93.59s; full yarn test:ci passed in 313.64s including security, both profiles, both todoapp builds, and 3 Playwright journeys per profile. Deferred scope is explicitly documented; no remaining work is required for this Bead's narrow accepted boundary.
close_reason: Closed typed package-import subset landed with three-runtime and full-CI evidence; broader package declaration shapes remain intentionally fail-closed.

title: Do not emit reserved any as a classic declaration alias
description: Classic declaration emission has special fallback accessors for tink.streams.RegroupStatus and RegroupResult. When those enum modules are materialized as declarations, the fallback is applied to the declaration name itself and produces illegal TypeScript such as export declare type any, followed by free generic names. This surfaced while a strict consumer temporarily imported the broad TestEnum test harness; the focused constructor-generic fixture was isolated so genes-3r3 could remain scoped.
acceptance_criteria: Classic declaration aliases always retain a legal declared identifier; unsupported type-position fallbacks never replace a declaration LHS; a dependency-isolated strict TypeScript consumer exercises the affected tink enum modules on TS 5/6/7 without skipLibCheck; both compiler profiles and full CI remain green.
notes: Implemented a declaration-position-safe base-type emitter. Classic declaration aliases and genes-ts declaration identities now always keep their real collision-safe names and generic parameters, while the existing RegroupStatus/RegroupResult fallback remains limited to type-reference positions. Added an isolated strict consumer that rejects reserved 'type any' declarations and compiles the affected generic unions with TypeScript 5.5.4, 6.0.2, and 7.0.2. Verification: yarn test:classic:dts; yarn test:genes-ts:full (310 assertions); yarn test (311 assertions); yarn test:ci (green in 265.03s, including dual output, acceptance/Playwright, and ts2hx).
close_reason: Declaration identities now bypass legacy type-position fallbacks, and isolated TS 5/6/7 coverage plus full CI prove legal classic aliases without changing existing type-reference behavior.

title: Preserve constructor-local generic enum payloads in classic declarations
description: Classic .d.ts output still widens payloads that use a constructor-local enum type parameter, such as Gen.More<T>(c:T), because the constructor variant type does not declare T. genes-ts already models these parameters with a never default. This is distinct from the nullary enum-parameter fix and needs its own generic declaration design and consumer coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Classic variant aliases declare constructor-local type parameters without any; constructor functions and union uses remain assignable; genes-ts behavior stays unchanged; strict TS 5/6/7 consumers and classic runtime pass; full CI is green.
notes: Implemented a shared enum-constructor type-parameter printer used by classic declarations and genes-ts. Classic variant aliases now declare constructor-local parameters with preserved constraints and a never default, and payload fields retain those precise names instead of any. Added a dependency-free Haxe fixture, exact declaration assertions, and an external strict negative consumer. Verified with yarn test:classic:dts (TS 5.5/6/7), yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test (311/311), and yarn test:ci (263.58s). Follow-up genes-1mk owns the unrelated pre-existing reserved any alias fallback discovered during fixture isolation.
close_reason: Classic constructor-local enum payloads are strongly typed, covered across TS 5/6/7, and full CI is green.

title: Publish ESM runtime request compatibility evidence
description: Update semantic rows, counts, portability wording, CLI/manifest docs, snapshot target inventory, and compatibility reports for the split between binding translation and Genes-only effective runtime requests.
design: Derive counts and per-fixture target claims from tracked owners. Do not claim standard-Haxe runtime parity for generated files requiring the Genes request capability.
acceptance_criteria: Docs, semantic matrices, generated compatibility artifacts, and fixture inventories agree with executable owners; all focused gates and full yarn test:ci pass; the parent decision Bead can close.
notes: Published the ordered ESM request contract as a separate modules.esm-runtime-requests supported-with-helper/J1 feature. The machine catalog now owns 19 rows (17 supported, 2 unsupported) and 13 canonical fail-closed boundaries, including the standard-Haxe target diagnostic. Feature provenance is compared exactly with configured TypeScript runtime requests in verbatim and non-verbatim projects. Runtime-profile evidence proves request-free standard Haxe, transactional wrong-target rejection, the 11 Genes / 9 standard / 8 standard-runtime snapshot inventory, and Genes-to-genes.disable capability isolation on one Haxe compile server. Architecture, output-mode, portability, CLI, limitation, roadmap, and compatibility artifacts now describe the same boundary without claiming standard-Haxe parity. Verified with yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test (63.30s) and pinned-Node yarn test:ci (256.74s).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fail closed and expand ESM request binding shapes
description: Add focused evidence and stable boundaries for default, namespace, empty, mixed type/value, package-bound, mutable live bindings, converted cycles, runtime re-exports, attributes, and module format.
design: Promote one shape at a time only after original TypeScript and both Genes profiles agree. Keep live bindings, runtime re-exports, and converted SCCs unsupported until dedicated semantic differentials prove TDZ and mutation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Every supported shape has a three-runtime differential and full-DCE output assertions; every deferred shape has a stable source-positioned strict diagnostic, assisted loss record, and transaction-preservation test.
notes: Implemented ordered planning for effective ESM runtime requests. Direct, acyclic converted imports now cover immutable named, default, namespace, empty, mixed type/value, and combined clauses using exact post-TypeScript-transform bindings. Package-bound requests, mutable/live bindings, CommonJS module output, converted cycles, runtime re-exports, and converted import attributes fail closed with stable source-positioned diagnostics and transactional strict output. Verified with focused request-plan tests, snapshots, three-runtime full-DCE semantic differentials, compatibility evidence, strict diagnostics, and full yarn test:ci (277.80s).
close_reason: Closed

title: Preserve all supported effective ESM requests in Genes
description: Generate ordered compiler-internal carriers for every supported effective TypeScript runtime request, including standalone bound-only modules and unused emitted imports, while both Genes profiles consume the existing shared request projection.
design: Start with acyclic converted immutable named/alias imports. Attach live bindings to first-occurrence request slots, retain request-only modules through full DCE, and avoid printer-specific ordering logic.
acceptance_criteria: Original TypeScript, classic Genes, and genes-ts match repro A and B with verbatim on/off under full DCE; emitted request order is exact; duplicate requests execute once; no carrier, marker, token, metadata, or declaration surface leaks.
notes: Implemented configured-emit-driven ordered carriers for standalone acyclic converted named/aliased imports whose runtime bindings resolve to immutable const exports. The planner now consumes EffectiveModuleRequestInventory in emit order, omits TypeScript-elided imports, retains verbatim unused imports, closes the supported converted dependency graph, and leaves both Genes printers on the shared DependencyPlan projection. Added original-TypeScript/classic-Genes/genes-ts full-DCE differentials for reverse value-use order, aliasing, an unused verbatim request, duplicate request coalescing, and JS/TS/d.ts leakage checks. Reviewed snapshot delta: roundtrip-advanced Todo now owns one compiler-internal clamp request carrier. Focused tools/ts2hx test passed in 83.17s; full yarn test:ci passed in 368.44s. Default/namespace/package bindings, mutable live bindings, cycles, runtime re-exports, and generalized fail-closed boundaries remain intentionally deferred to genes-blt.4; final matrix/report publication remains genes-blt.5.
close_reason: Supported immutable converted named/alias requests now preserve configured TypeScript request order in both Genes profiles with full CI evidence.

title: Add explicit ts2hx ESM runtime capability profile
description: Introduce an explicit Genes ESM versus standard-Haxe runtime profile, compiler capability metadata, and transactional target diagnostics so generated request carriers never compile and crash under the wrong generator.
design: Depend on the effective-request inventory. Prefer a compiler-internal macro target guard over conditional source omission. Version the manifest deliberately and preserve the runtimeModules resource-ownership field.
acceptance_criteria: The Genes profile records its required compiler capability; the standard-Haxe profile fails at the first effective request with stable source provenance and an unchanged prior output tree; request-free standard-Haxe fixtures remain covered.
notes: Implemented schema-v3 target capability planning. Every emitting API/CLI caller now selects genes-esm or standard-haxe-js; effective TypeScript requests record original provenance, final disposition/order/format, the exact @typescript/typescript6@6.0.2 bridge and TypeScript 6.0.3 engine, an options hash, and genes.esm-runtime-requests while preserving runtimeModules. The request-free standard profile fails at the first retained request with TS2HX-MODULES-ESM-RUNTIME-TARGET-001, remains an error in assisted mode, and leaves prior output byte-identical. Generated carriers now pass through a documented EsmRequestFact macro that reports GENES-ESM-REQUEST-TARGET-001 under the wrong Haxe generator. Snapshot ownership is explicit: 11 genes-esm fixtures and 9 request-free standard fixtures, with 8 standard runtime smokes. Legacy fixtures were made clean under pinned NodeNext diagnostics so request evidence never depends on an invalid TS program. Full yarn test:ci passed under Node 20 in 256.79s, including both todoapp Playwright profiles and the complete ts2hx suite. Ordered carriers for every effective bound-only request remain intentionally deferred to genes-blt.3; additional binding shapes and final compatibility publication remain genes-blt.4/.5.
close_reason: Closed

title: Define bound-only ESM request order across output targets
description: The side-effect-import fix carries ordered request plans through converted runtime closures seeded by bare imports. A translated entry/module containing only bound runtime imports can still let Genes discover dependencies from Haxe value-use order rather than TypeScript declaration order. Generating compiler-internal carriers for every bound-only file fixes Genes but breaks the established standard-Haxe snapshot/runtime contract because SideEffectImportMarker is Genes-only. Reduce the standalone bound-only case, decide whether modules.esm-bindings promises declaration-order initialization, and design a target-polymorphic or explicit capability boundary before changing production behavior.
design: This is broader than genes-bxw. Per AGENTS.md, prepare a focused GPT 5.6 Pro architecture prompt before implementation because conditional/no-op carriers risk silently deleting initialization semantics while unconditional carriers break standard Haxe. Include the reduced A-then-B imports / B-then-A value-use repro and the closure-based fix as baseline.
acceptance_criteria: A documented decision states the exact modules.esm-bindings order contract; if supported, original TS, classic Genes, genes-ts, and every retained standard-Haxe claim have focused evidence with no marker leakage or snapshot surprise; if unsupported, strict diagnostics and matrix/docs accurately fail closed.
notes: Architecture decision completed across genes-blt.1-.5 using the GPT-5.6 Pro review. Configured TypeScript emit now owns whether each static import produces a runtime request; supported effective ESM requests retain source order across classic Genes and genes-ts under full DCE. The first supported converted subset covers immutable named/default/namespace, empty, mixed, and combined clauses. Package-bound imports, mutable live bindings, converted SCCs, runtime re-exports, converted attributes, and non-ESM output fail closed. Standard Haxe is explicitly request-free and rejects effective requests transactionally; generated carrier macros repeat that guard, including under same-server reuse. Public semantic, portability, fixture, and compatibility evidence now reflects 17 supported rows, 2 unsupported rows, and 13 named failures. Full pinned-Node CI passed in 256.74s.
close_reason: Closed

title: Run ts2hx once in the aggregate CI gate
description: The root test:ci command invokes test:acceptance without SKIP_TS2HX, so acceptance runs the complete ts2hx suite and the following dedicated test:ts2hx command runs the same suite again. GitHub split lanes already avoid this duplication.
design: Set SKIP_TS2HX=1 only for the acceptance invocation inside test:ci, retain the immediately following dedicated test:ts2hx gate, and document the composition. Do not weaken standalone test:acceptance, which remains the complete user-facing acceptance command by default.
acceptance_criteria: Root yarn test:ci runs acceptance with SKIP_CLASSIC=1 and SKIP_TS2HX=1, then runs one dedicated ts2hx suite; standalone yarn test:acceptance still includes ts2hx by default; full yarn test:ci passes with unchanged semantic coverage and a single ts2hx execution.
close_reason: Root aggregate CI now skips acceptance's embedded ts2hx pass and runs the dedicated ts2hx suite exactly once afterward. Full yarn test:ci passed in 275.71s, including dual-output examples and both todoapp Playwright profiles.

title: Make the dual-output example manifest executable
description: examples/profiles.json lists TS/classic build, runtime, and browser commands, but scripts/test-examples.ts ignores those fields and hardcodes the current two examples. That lets a newly declared directory satisfy inventory checks without being built or executed. Make the checked-in manifest the actual command owner for every example/profile.
design: Upgrade the manifest to structured command/argument records and have test-examples validate and execute them with execFileSync (never a shell). Require ts-strict and classic-esm for every example, require symmetric browser ownership, deduplicate identical build contracts, preserve --skip-todoapp, and select runtime versus Playwright commands explicitly. Keep source roots at example scope so profiles cannot fork Haxe sources.
acceptance_criteria: Every declared example/profile is built and runtime-smoked from its manifest command; --playwright executes each declared browser contract; malformed/missing/asymmetric command records fail before execution; identical build commands are deduplicated; todoapp TS and classic profiles retain one shared Haxe source root and the same API/browser journeys; focused examples tests and full yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Upgraded examples/profiles.json to a structured executable command schema and made test-examples validate every example/profile before running shell-free build and QA commands. Identical builders are deduplicated, browser ownership is symmetric, and every TS/classic runtime contract executes. Verified yarn test:examples (4 profiles) and full yarn test:ci in 328.15s, including three Playwright journeys each for TS and direct classic todoapp output.

title: Keep architecture evidence counts executable
description: The architecture roadmap contains stale hand-maintained ts2hx evidence counts after semantic contracts were promoted. Correct the current snapshot/support/fail-closed inventory and extend the existing ts2hx documentation gate so readiness prose is derived from the same semantic matrices and snapshot owners as executable tests.
design: Have test-docs read docs/ARCHITECTURE_ROADMAP.md and require the current snapshot-file count, supported-row count, unsupported-row count, and fail-closed-case count. Phrase the roadmap so unsupported feature rows and supported-feature limitations remain distinct. Do not add a second inventory or parse TypeScript source with regex.
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE_ROADMAP reports 48 snapshot files, 15 supported semantic rows, 3 unsupported rows, and 4 exercised fail-closed variants; test:docs fails if any count drifts; repository search finds no superseded 14/4/53 claim; focused docs/ts2hx tests and full yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Corrected stale architecture evidence to 48 reviewed snapshots, 15 supported semantic rows, 3 unsupported rows, and 4 exercised fail-closed variants. Extended test:docs to derive and enforce all four roadmap counts from the snapshot tree and semantic matrices. Verified full ts2hx suite and yarn test:ci (351.31s), including TS/classic todoapp E2E.

title: Model switch-to-enclosing-loop continue in ts2hx
description: ts2hx currently rejects every unlabelled continue that escapes a lowered switch, even though its switch state machine and enclosing-loop continue-step stack contain enough information to preserve the transfer. Promote the common unlabelled form without letting continue target the synthetic do/while used for switch break/fallthrough.
design: Add an explicit switch ContinueTransfer plan/context. A continue at the switch's enclosing loop depth sets a generated transfer flag and breaks the synthetic switch loop; after that loop, propagate to an enclosing synthetic switch or emit the real loop continue, including a for increment exactly once. Nested real loops retain their own continue target. Keep labeled continue fail closed with the stable switch.continue diagnostic. Do not use exceptions, Dynamic, untyped, raw JS, or string post-processing.
acceptance_criteria: Original TypeScript, translated classic JS, and genes-ts TS->JS traces match for unlabelled switch continue in while and for loops, including conditionals, nested switches, nested real loops, fallthrough, break, and exact for-step counts; switch.continue is supported/P0 with provenance; labeled switch continue remains a precise transactional strict failure; generated Haxe exposes explicit transfer flags and contains no weak types; docs distinguish 15 supported rows, 3 unsupported rows, and 4 fail-closed variants; focused ts2hx tests and full yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Implemented explicit switch-to-enclosing-loop continue transfer planning. Unlabelled continue now preserves while/for semantics, nested switches propagate correctly, nested real loops stay local, for steps run exactly once, and labeled continue remains a source-positioned strict failure. Verified by 15-contract/4-fail-closed three-runtime semantic differential, full ts2hx suite, and yarn test:ci (363.10s), including TS/classic todoapp E2E.

title: Promote ts2hx unary-plus coercion to an exact strict-js contract
description: ts2hx currently rejects TypeScript unary plus even though the strict-js pipeline already has a semantic support matrix and three-runtime differential harness. Add a generic, typed genes.js coercion boundary and lower unary plus through it without weakening translated Haxe or generated TypeScript. Preserve JavaScript ToNumber behavior for the supported TypeScript operand surface and keep the remaining unsupported semantic cases fail closed.
design: Use a named target-polymorphic Haxe helper whose macro expansion confines js.Syntax.code to the helper contract while preserving the original operand type at the emitted operator site. Record coercion.unary-plus as supported-with-helper/J1. Do not add Dynamic, untyped, downstream-specific behavior, or a raw string rewrite in ts2hx.
acceptance_criteria: coercion.unary-plus is supported-with-helper/J1 with stable provenance; the semantic differential exercises numeric, empty, whitespace, invalid, signed, and single-evaluation string coercions across original TypeScript, classic Genes JS, and genes-ts TS->JS; generated Haxe uses the named helper; strict TypeScript remains clean; semantic-unsupported retains exactly the other four precise diagnostics and transactional-output proof; ts2hx docs/counts are updated; focused ts2hx tests and full yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Implemented typed macro-erased genes.js.Coercion.toNumber lowering; 14 three-runtime semantic contracts and four fail-closed contracts pass; full yarn test:ci passed in 299.64s.

title: Make compiler output tree transactional
description: The compiler preflights dependency and JSX capability errors and buffers each generated file, but implementation modules, source maps, StdTypes, and declarations are still committed one file at a time. A later emitter failure can therefore leave a mixed old/new output tree.
design: Add explicit output ownership and a compilation-level staging directory around the existing emitters. Emitters keep producing buffered files; Generator commits the staged owned tree only after every implementation/declaration pass succeeds. Preserve unrelated user files, handle platform-specific rename behavior deterministically, and keep TS/classic/declaration semantics out of the filesystem layer.
acceptance_criteria: A forced failure after multiple planned modules leaves the prior output tree byte-identical; a successful build atomically publishes implementation files, source maps, StdTypes, and declarations; stale compiler-owned files are removed without deleting unrelated files; TS and classic failure/success fixtures pass; determinism and full yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Compiler output now uses same-filesystem private staging, catchable generation diagnostics, per-entrypoint ownership manifests, stale owned-file cleanup, Haxe output isolation, and rollback across TS/classic/declarations/maps. TS/classic fault-injection, dual-output, examples, and full yarn test:ci pass.

title: Add opt-in reusable-library profile with matched JS and declaration surfaces
description: Default classic app builds must keep Haxe runtime DCE honest: a declaration cannot promise a class method absent from emitted JavaScript. Add an explicit reusable-library profile that retains a complete selected public class surface in both classic JS and declarations without broadening default output.
design: Treat public runtime retention and declaration emission as one profile contract. Reuse PublicSurface and DependencyPlan, but model abstract-instance APIs separately from KAbstractImpl static ownership so owner type parameters never leak into invalid static declarations. Do not remove the default post-DCE class-member intersection.
acceptance_criteria: An opt-in profile retains selected public class methods and every type they reference in both emitted JS and strict declarations; the default classic-esm/classic-dts profile remains DCE-compact; abstract-instance generic fixtures avoid static owner-generic leakage; paired runtime and strict consumer gates plus yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Landed an opt-in genes.library profile that preserves selected public runtime and declaration surfaces in classic JS and TypeScript, keeps default DCE compact, handles abstract ownership generics correctly, and passes the full CI plus paired runtime/strict-consumer gates.

title: Extract TempPlan NamePlan and evaluation-order lowering
description: Extract temporary allocation, collision-safe naming, and evaluation-order-sensitive expression shapes from mutable printer state into per-function plans and a deliberately small LoweredExpr/LoweredStmt layer.
design: Do not create a universal Haxe target AST. Normalize only constructs whose shape must survive more than one pass: expression-valued control flow, side-effecting receivers/keys, assignments, short-circuiting, loops, and source-map-sensitive temporaries. Require semantic traces and output budgets before switching printers.
acceptance_criteria: Curated evaluation-order traces match standard Haxe JS/current classic/TS output as applicable; no-temp fixtures gain no unnecessary declarations; name collisions remain deterministic; printers allocate no semantic temporaries for migrated constructs; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Extracted immutable TempPlan and NamePlan, migrated for-loop/value-expression lowering out of printer allocation, documented ownership and evidence, and passed full CI including dual-output and both todoapp browser profiles.

title: Generate compatibility evidence and downstream QA manifests
description: Publish deterministic evidence that separates compile inventory, strict public typing, semantic differential, snapshot, smoke, toolchain, package-shape, and downstream results. Use pinned PiMonoHX/OpenCodeHX curated contracts as nightly pressure tests.
design: Every downstream compiler defect must reduce to a generic genes fixture before blocking core work. Full evolving applications remain nonblocking nightly/release evidence until stability data justifies promotion.
acceptance_criteria: Generated report contains no timestamps/local paths, never conflates smoke with semantic parity, links exact fixture counts/revisions, and reports compiler/downstream/unsupported status separately; curated no-network downstream jobs are pinned and reproducible.
close_reason: Published deterministic eight-class compatibility evidence with exact counts and pinned revisions; added validated no-network PiMonoHX/OpenCodeHX nightly contracts with separated ownership/status, documented the bounded claims, and passed full CI including both todoapp profiles.

title: Bridge dts2hx ingestion with shared package-shape fixtures
description: Use dts2hx as the declaration-ingestion partner for npm ecosystems while keeping its conversion implementation separate from genes-ts and ts2hx implementation-source migration.
design: Share versioned ESM/CommonJS/conditional-export/subpath/type-value fixture contracts and a small TS compiler-API adapter boundary. Generated externs carry reproducible package/version/loss manifests.
acceptance_criteria: Selected ESM, export-equals CommonJS, conditional export, and subpath packages generate externs deterministically, compile in Haxe, and produce correct genes TS/classic imports without downstream Dynamic workarounds.
close_reason: Landed deterministic dts2hx 0.34.0 declaration-ingestion bridge with versioned ESM, CommonJS export-equals, conditional-export, subpath, type/value, reproducibility, strict typing, TS/classic runtime, and loss-manifest coverage; yarn test:ci passes.

title: Build ts2hx semantic IR and differential support matrix
description: Incrementally replace ts2hx's behavior-changing direct string lowerings with a minimal semantic IR for undefined/null/absence, evaluation order, control flow, modules, support grades, and source provenance.
design: Build on the landed fail-closed diagnostic/transaction boundary. Migrate one risk category at a time; do not clone the full TypeScript AST. Add strict-portable only after its contract is evidence-backed.
acceptance_criteria: Printers make no support decisions for migrated categories; original TS versus translated Haxe-to-JS differentials cover defaults, uninitialized values, truthiness, evaluation order, loops/continue, switch, exceptions/finally, this/prototypes, async, and modules; support manifests grade each feature.
notes: Completed with schema-v2 semantic support/provenance manifests, 13 exact contracts across original TS, translated classic JS, and translated genes-ts output, plus 5 fail-closed unsupported contracts. Full yarn test:ci passed in 248.04s, including TS and classic todoapp E2E.
close_reason: Implemented schema-v2 semantic support/provenance manifests, 13 exact contracts across original TS, translated classic JS, and translated genes-ts output, plus 5 fail-closed unsupported contracts. Full yarn test:ci passed in 248.04s, including TS and classic todoapp E2E.

title: Make StdTypes WebIDL globals package-safe
description: Downstream multi-package consumers can import two genes-ts-generated packages in one TypeScript program. Each package currently ships StdTypes.d.ts with identical ambient const declarations for WebIDL gap values such as PositionError and FetchObserver, causing TS2451 duplicate block-scoped variable errors under skipLibCheck:false. Fix StdTypesEmitter generically so repeated generated support modules merge cleanly without weakening Haxe JS std/runtime metadata declarations.
notes: Changed StdTypesEmitter WebIDL value gaps from ambient const to ambient var so duplicated generated StdTypes.d.ts modules merge across multi-package consumers. Added genes-ts regression that imports two copied StdTypes support modules with skipLibCheck:false. Focused gate passed: yarn test:genes-ts.
close_reason: Fixed StdTypes WebIDL globals to emit mergeable ambient var declarations, refreshed generated snapshots/examples, and added a multi-package StdTypes.d.ts merge regression. Verified with yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci.

title: Fix genes-ts nullable Map.get emission
description: OpenCodeHX exposed a generic genes-ts regression after StdTypes emission refactor: generated TypeScript now types Map.get as nullable but emits plain map.get(...) at sites where Haxe control flow proves presence, such as exists checks and iteration over map keys. Add generic fixtures and restore strict-checkable output without downstream-specific knowledge.
close_reason: accepted: value-position Map.get narrowing emits non-null assertions after exists/key iteration, with no-js-es fixture assertions and full yarn test:ci passing

title: genes-ts: move StdTypes emission into dedicated TS stdlib emitter
description: The Date metadata quick fix added TypeScript global augmentations inline in Generator.hx. Refactor StdTypes.ts emission into a dedicated genes.ts.StdTypesEmitter so Haxe structural types, Haxe runtime metadata globals, WebIDL gaps, and value-level stubs have clear ownership. Keep generated artifact stable and add String instance metadata for Haxe JS boot String.prototype.__class__.
acceptance_criteria: Generator.hx delegates StdTypes generation to genes.ts.StdTypesEmitter; generated StdTypes.ts includes a named HxRuntimeName alias plus String/StringConstructor/ArrayConstructor/Date/DateConstructor metadata augmentations; Date.now().getTime() fixture still typechecks; focused genes-ts snapshot/type/runtime gates pass; PiMonoHX build and compaction smoke pass after pin update.
close_reason: Moved StdTypes.ts support emission into genes.ts.StdTypesEmitter, added narrow Haxe JS runtime metadata augmentations including String.__class__, regenerated snapshots, and validated with yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test, plus PiMonoHX npm run ci:full at pin a5610b0.

title: genes-ts: declare Haxe boot metadata on Date globals
description: PiMonoHX compaction exposed a strict TypeScript failure when normal Haxe Date usage caused generated Std.ts to assign Date.prototype.__class__ and Date.__name__. StdTypes already augments StringConstructor and ArrayConstructor for Haxe boot metadata, but Date is missing. Add Date/DateConstructor global augmentations so downstream code can use typed Haxe Date APIs instead of Syntax.code workarounds.
acceptance_criteria: A genes-ts fixture or snapshot that emits Haxe Date boot metadata typechecks under strict TypeScript; generated StdTypes includes Date and DateConstructor metadata augmentations; focused genes-ts gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: Date/DateConstructor Haxe boot metadata globals are declared in genes-ts StdTypes, snapshot fixtures cover normal Date.now().getTime() use, todoapp generated snapshots are synchronized, and gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test; git diff --check; yarn test:ci.

title: Emit @:ts.optional object-field values as undefined at TS boundary
description: After adding @:ts.optional declaration emission, PiMonoHX exposed implementation TS errors where object literals assigned Haxe nullable optional-field reads (x.field ?? null) into fields whose TS contract is field?: T. Extend TS source emission so object literals targeting anonymous fields marked @:ts.optional normalize nullable field values to undefined at the boundary, preserving Haxe source null reads while satisfying the generated TS contract.
acceptance_criteria: A generic genes-ts snapshot covers @:ts.optional object literals built from nullable optional parameters/field reads and emits value ?? undefined rather than value ?? null for the optional boundary field. Existing @:optional fields without @:ts.optional keep null-normalized reads. Focused genes-ts snapshots and tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented TS source emission support for @:ts.optional anonymous fields. Object literals targeting marked fields now normalize nullable-producing Haxe values to undefined at the TS boundary, while fresh literals/functions/arrays and unmarked optional fields keep existing behavior. Added snapshot coverage for nullable parameters and optional field reads copied into @:ts.optional records. Verified: yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test:versions; yarn test; SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance; yarn test:ts2hx.

title: Add @:ts.optional anonymous field marker
description: PiMonoHX and other TS-facing Haxe projects need to express the common JavaScript object-property contract `field?: T` while keeping normal Haxe optional-field reads as `Null<T>`. `genes.ts.Undefinable<T>` is precise for values that can be undefined, and field-level `@:ts.type` can override types, but neither is ergonomic as a broad DTO annotation. Add a generic anonymous-field metadata marker that strips only the generated optional-field null union in TS output without changing Haxe source typing.
acceptance_criteria: A generic snapshot fixture demonstrates `@:ts.optional` on anonymous optional fields for string, array, function, nested typedef, and enum/literal-ish fields. Generated TS source and .d.ts emit `field?: T` without `| null`; unmarked optional fields still emit `field?: T | null`; existing gates remain green.
close_reason: Implemented @:ts.optional for anonymous typedef fields. Generated TS now emits field?: T without | null for marked optional fields while unmarked optional fields keep conservative null unions. Added snapshot coverage for string, array, function, nested typedef, and enum abstract literal fields; SignatureCache strips the optional-field Null wrapper before caching literal unions. Verified: yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test:versions; yarn test; SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance; yarn test:ts2hx. Full yarn test:ci still stops at the known yarn audit advisory tracked by genes-2wl for @sigstore/core GHSA-jfc7-64v2-mr8c.

title: Support @:ts.type on anonymous typedef fields
description: Anonymous typedef fields currently preserve enum-abstract literal unions through SignatureCache, but field-level `@:ts.type` metadata is not captured for generated TypeScript source or declaration output. Downstream DTOs need to keep ergonomic Haxe `@:optional field:T` semantics while emitting canonical TypeScript field types such as `field?: T` instead of `field?: T | null` without wrapping source fields in `Undefinable<T>`.
acceptance_criteria: A generic snapshot fixture demonstrates `@:ts.type` on anonymous typedef fields, including optional fields and nested/function/array examples. Generated TS source and .d.ts output use the override exactly at the field boundary. Existing genes-ts and classic JS gates remain green.
notes: Implemented generic anonymous typedef field metadata support in TypeEmitter: field-level `@:ts.type`/`@:genes.type` now overrides the emitted TypeScript field type before inferred optional-null emission or literal-union cache. Added a BoundaryTypes snapshot fixture with optional string, readonly array, function, and nested-record fields that keep normal Haxe `?field:T` source ergonomics while generating `field?: T`-style TypeScript. Verification: yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots passed; yarn test:genes-ts passed; yarn test:ci failed only at the known @sigstore/core OSV item tracked by genes-2wl; remaining CI legs passed manually with yarn test:versions, yarn test, SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance, and yarn test:ts2hx.
close_reason: Implemented generic @:ts.type/@:genes.type overrides for anonymous typedef fields with snapshot coverage.

title: genes-ts: emit computed native member calls without dot
description: Calling a Haxe method annotated with @:native("[Symbol.asyncIterator]") currently emits invalid TypeScript member access like stream.[Symbol.asyncIterator](). Computed native member access should emit stream[Symbol.asyncIterator](), preserving typed Symbol-based interop without raw JS.
acceptance_criteria: A generic genes-ts repro exists and fails on the current invalid stream.[Symbol.asyncIterator]() output, or the compiler is fixed and the repro passes.
notes:
Added expected-failing repro at tests/genes-ts/repros/computed-native-member-call. Current output defines the method correctly as [Symbol.asyncIterator](): AsyncIterator<number>, but a Haxe call emits invalid TypeScript at out/src-gen/Main.ts:27: stream.[Symbol.asyncIterator]().next(). The default yarn test:genes-ts suite still passes; the repro fails intentionally with TS1003 until computed native member access is fixed.
Updated repro source to use module-level nextFromNative/main functions instead of a static-only Main shell, matching the Haxe authoring rule to prefer module-level functions when no class identity is needed.
close_reason: The checked-in computed native member call repro is now an ordinary passing test invoked by test:genes-ts; full yarn test:ci passed.

title: genes-ts: preserve discriminated typedef unions in emitted TS
description: PiMonoHX DTOs reduce to weak TypeScript declarations for tagged unions: typedef aliases such as Message/AssistantMessageEvent emit as any, and discriminants such as role/type/stopReason emit as string instead of string-literal unions. Create/fix a generic compiler fixture for Haxe typedef unions and enum-abstract-backed discriminants so generated TS is at least as ergonomic and safe as equivalent handwritten TS.
notes: Added expected-failing repro at tests/genes-ts/repros/discriminated-unions. Command: node tests/genes-ts/repros/discriminated-unions/check.mjs. Current failure: generated Main.ts emits TextMessage.role and ToolMessage.role as string. Desired output keeps singleton enum-abstract discriminants as TextOnlyRole/ToolOnlyRole with literal aliases "text"/"tool", and declaration output preserves the same shape. The reduced EitherType alias emits Message = TextMessage | ToolMessage here, so the first compiler gap is literal discriminant preservation in genes-ts source output.
close_reason: The checked-in discriminated-union repro now preserves literal discriminants and is invoked by test:genes-ts; full yarn test:ci passed.

Reduce this to a generic class/inheritance declaration fixture and replace or hide the helper-base any without depending on downstream module names.

acceptance_criteria: A generic genes-ts TSX fixture covers nested children/component assembly; generated TSX avoids large tmp/tmpN child-element clusters where readable names or direct expression emission are possible; TypeScript TSX checks and full compiler gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: added generic react TSX fixture for lowered child locals; tag-based local preferences remove tmp/tmpN child clusters in generated TSX while preserving declaration order. Verified yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test, yarn test:acceptance, and yarn test:ci.

title: genes-ts: preserve closed enum abstract unions in array loop locals
description: Discovered from OpenCodeHX opencodehx-7lj. A closed enum abstract array literal such as [Config, Plugin] iterated with for (id in ...) generated TypeScript loop locals typed as plain string. Passing that local to a function expecting the closed enum abstract literal union then failed strict TypeScript. This is a generic closed enum abstract/literal-array inference issue, not downstream-specific behavior.

title: genes-ts: tighten typed catch lowering
description: Discovered from a downstream typed catch over a user exception class. Haxe typed catches lower through haxe.Exception.caught(...).unwrap(), and genes-ts currently emits a user-module local typed as any before the runtime type guard. Reduce this generically so typed catches over user classes avoid broad generated any in user modules or confine any to a documented runtime helper.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture covers catch(error:UserExceptionClass); generated TS avoids explicit user-module any for the caught local; TypeScript output strict-checks; full yarn test:ci passes or any unrelated external failure is documented.
close_reason: accepted: typed catch lowering now detects haxe.Exception.caught(...).unwrap() locals and emits the lowered catch temporary as {} | null | undefined instead of user-module any, preserving TypeScript instanceof/typeof narrowing. Added a generic snapshot fixture for catch(error:FixtureError) with fallback catch and runtime output. Gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts; UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts:full; yarn test; yarn test:ci; git diff --check.

title: genes-ts: guard map set temp declarations in no-js-es output
description: Discovered from a downstream provider-registry smoke. Inline Haxe Map.set lowering can introduce repeated locals named value in the same generated TypeScript function; under var-scoped output, a later set with a different value type fails strict tsc with TS2403. Genes should cover this generically without downstream source workarounds.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal genes-ts fixture that performs two Map.set calls with different value record types in the same function under the no-js-es TypeScript profile; generated TS must not emit duplicate var-scoped value declarations and must pass strict tsc; yarn test:genes-ts and yarn test:ci pass.
notes: Reopened after downstream rebuild still reproduced TS2403. The first fixture covered IMap call output, but the actual failing path is inline expansion of genes.util.EsMap.set(key, value), where repeated inline parameter locals named value can be emitted in the same generated TS function with different types.
close_reason: accepted: fixed generic genes-ts local naming for inline-expanded Map.set/value temps. Final fixture in tests/genes-ts/no-js-es uses escaping Haxe Map values with two different record types and asserts value/value_1 suffixing; snapshots updated for valid duplicate-local suffixes in Boot and todoapp examples. Gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn build:example:todoapp, git diff --check, yarn test:ci.

Added BoundaryTypes guardedCall and guardedOptionalItemsCall fixtures; generated TS emits direct value / field non-null assertion instead of Register.unsafeCast. title: genes-ts: use abstract underlying anonymous fields for object context
description: Discovered after fixing direct ternary expected-type propagation. A downstream options object returns an abstract with @:ts.type over an underlying anonymous Haxe shape. Object-literal field emission could not recover the underlying field type, so Undefinable<T> field branches still emitted null instead of undefined. Fix this generically by using abstract-underlying anonymous fields for object-literal field names/types where safe.

- Add a minimal genes-ts fixture for an abstract over an anonymous shape with a raw TS type override and Undefinable<T> field
- Returning an object literal through that abstract emits undefined in a conditional missing branch and strict-checks

title: genes-ts: propagate expected types into conditional branches
description: Discovered from a downstream provider options factory. A Haxe object field typed as genes.ts.Undefinable<Bool> used a conditional expression like nullable == null ? Undefinable.absent() : nullable. The object field expected type reached the conditional expression, but not its branches, so generated TypeScript emitted null instead of undefined in a boolean | undefined field. Fix this generically by carrying expected value type through TIf/ternary branch emission.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a minimal genes-ts fixture for an object field typed Undefinable<T> whose value is a conditional expression over a nullable local
- Generated TypeScript emits undefined on the absent branch and strict-checks without casts
- Existing direct return/assignment/array Undefinable fixtures still pass
- yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test, and git diff --check pass or any unrelated gate failure is documented
close_reason: accepted: expected type now propagates into conditional-expression branches; fixture emits boolean | undefined object field as enabled == null ? undefined : enabled. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts:full; yarn test; git diff --check. Full test:ci remains separately blocked by genes-02l vulnerability refresh.

title: genes-ts: elide null-guarded local unsafeCast
description: A downstream usage-mapping reduction exposed a generic output-quality issue: after a Haxe null guard, assigning a Null<T> local to T emits Register.unsafeCast<T>(value) even though the generated TypeScript can narrow the local naturally. Reduce this in BoundaryTypes and elide the cast only when the expression is a stable local proven non-null in the current branch.
acceptance_criteria: BoundaryTypes or an equivalent fixture covers a null-guarded Null<T> local assigned to T and generated TS uses the local directly without Register.unsafeCast; strict genes-ts gates and snapshots pass; behavior remains conservative for unproven casts.
close_reason: accepted: null-guarded nullable locals now carry non-null facts into guarded branches and the immediate post-early-return statement, eliding unnecessary Register.unsafeCast in generated TS. Added BoundaryTypes fixture coverage for Undefinable helper output; snapshots updated, including Register.global cast removal. Gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test:genes-ts:full; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test; git diff --check.

title: genes-ts: narrow optional fields through boolean conditions
description: Discovered from a downstream strict TypeScript build. A Haxe ternary like optional.field == null || optional.field == "" ? fallback : optional.field leaves the else branch non-null in Haxe, but genes-ts re-emitted the optional field as string | null in the generated TS object literal. Fix optional-field null facts generically across boolean conditions such as || and &&, without downstream-specific knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture covers an optional field guarded by a compound || condition and used in the non-null branch; generated TypeScript strict-checks without source workarounds; existing optional-field narrowing fixtures still pass; yarn test:genes-ts and snapshot/full gates pass.
close_reason: accepted: optional-field narrowing now carries non-null facts through boolean &&/|| conditions; fixture covers compound || guard plus non-null branch; gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test, git diff --check.

title: Document TS-minded Haxe authoring model

description: Capture the compiler goal that genes-specific TypeScript helper abstractions should remain valid Haxe and degrade/erase cleanly to classic ES6 output while preserving richer TypeScript types in genes-ts mode.

title: Preserve enum abstract literal unions in typedef field emission
description: genes-ts already emits enum abstract method return and argument positions as TypeScript literal unions in several paths, but typedef/object field emission can widen the same enum abstract values to their underlying primitive type. Reduce this to a generic fixture and route typedef members, class fields, array elements, and nested object fields through the same literal-union-aware type emission path where safe.

The same erasure can affect locals initialized from class fields or anonymous typedef fields. Need a generic genes-ts fixture and fix for enum abstract literal unions in locals/typedef fields.

A downstream project was unblocked by loosening statSync to Dynamic at the Node seam, but genes-ts should emit or import secondary extern return types correctly.

- Generated TS imports or qualifies the secondary extern type and strict-checks

title: genes-ts: narrow optional array fields after null guards
description: Discovered during a downstream project. Iterating optional array fields after a Haxe null guard still generated TS locals typed as string[] | null, then strict TS reported possibly-null loop variables and failed assignments to string[]. A downstream project was unblocked with Dynamic normalization helpers, but genes-ts should preserve Haxe null-guard narrowing for optional array fields or emit non-null temporaries.

- Add a minimal genes-ts fixture that iterates an optional Array<String> field after a null guard

title: Evaluate a Haxe-authored ts2hx bootstrap
description: Explore whether ts2hx could eventually be implemented in typed Haxe and compiled through Genes. The goal would be toolchain consolidation and a demanding real-world exercise of Genes, not a rewrite for its own sake. The current TypeScript implementation remains authoritative until an incremental prototype proves that the change reduces or preserves overall complexity.
design: Start with a narrow read-only spike around the TypeScript Program and TypeChecker APIs. Require a reproducible stage-0 build path, typed extern coverage without Dynamic or untyped, and side-by-side diagnostics, snapshot, transaction, runtime, determinism, and performance evidence. Prefer seam-by-seam migration with rollback points; reject a flag-day port or a replacement TypeScript parser/type checker.
acceptance_criteria: A written feasibility decision identifies a reproducible stage-0 bootstrap; measures typed TypeScript Compiler API interop; compares diagnostics, output, determinism, transactions, runtime semantics, and performance against the TypeScript implementation; and recommends either a bounded incremental experiment or a documented no-go. No production rewrite begins from this Bead alone.
notes:
Dependency clarification: Genes has no production/runtime or compiler-build dependency on ts2hx today. ts2hx uses Genes to compile generated Haxe, while root CI runs ts2hx as a downstream pressure test; that is a validation feedback loop, not a build cycle. A Haxe-authored translator must avoid creating a new build cycle by proving a pinned stage-0 or standard-Haxe bootstrap path.
Completed the bounded feasibility study without moving any production ts2hx seam. A repository canary now compares the same real TypeScript Program/TypeChecker facts through direct TypeScript, standard Haxe JS with the Genes generator disabled, classic Genes, and genes-ts. It proves exact type/diagnostic provenance, typed readonly and undefined boundaries with no Dynamic/untyped/casts/generated any or unknown, TS 5/6/7 full-library checking, byte-identical clean rebuilds, and report-only timing/output measurements. Production inventory measured 10 files, 7,964 lines, 797 ts.* references, and 174 distinct API members. A local dts2hx trial was rejected as a wholesale extern strategy after 34 unhandled-symbol errors, 1,241 files/30,281 lines, and 1,658 Dynamic occurrences. Decision: keep TypeScript authoritative; retain only the canary and allow a future read-only project.ts mirror if prerequisites are re-evaluated. Verification: yarn test:ts2hx-bootstrap; yarn test:versions; yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test:docs; yarn test:compatibility-report; full yarn test:ci green in 314.67s, including both todoapp profiles and six Playwright journeys.
close_reason: Feasibility decision and executable canary landed: typed Program/TypeChecker interop is viable, but a production Haxe rewrite remains unjustified until broader transaction, diagnostic, semantic, and complexity evidence exists.

acceptance_criteria: - Fixture spec documented (files + features + assertions)\n- Identifies required ts2hx emitter enhancements (types/expressions/imports)\n- Plan for output typing checks
close_reason: Updated docs/ts2hx/PLAN.md: added M7 roundtrip harness spec (fixture layout + required TS feature surface + output quality checks) and fixed beads tracking IDs.

title: ts2hx: implement roundtrip fixture project
description: Add a medium-complexity pure TypeScript fixture under tools/ts2hx/fixtures/roundtrip-fixture with an executable entry that validates behavior (throws on failure). No external deps.
acceptance_criteria: - Fixture builds with tsc NodeNext\n- Running the fixture exits 0 and prints a small success marker\n- Uses features called out in the design task
close_reason: Added tools/ts2hx/fixtures/roundtrip-fixture (NodeNext TS project) with self-contained assertions and stable success marker; builds and runs via tsc.

title: ts2hx: extend type/codegen support needed by roundtrip
description: Improve ts2hx emitter to handle the TS types/expressions used by the roundtrip fixture (e.g. arrays, generic type args, unions/literals) without devolving to Dynamic everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: - Added support is covered by snapshots\n- Roundtrip fixture no longer fails due to 'unsupported' stubs\n- Output TS after genes-ts has limited any/unknown
close_reason: Extended ts2hx emitter to cover the roundtrip fixture (template literals, JSON.stringify mapping, JS Error mapping, loop translation, array types/args). Roundtrip fixture snapshots+Haxe smoke now pass without unsupported stubs; output-quality checks tracked in genes-dhg.1.5.

title: ts2hx: roundtrip fixture harness (TS→Haxe→TS)
description: Implement the PRD M4 roundtrip fixture: a medium-complexity pure TS project is transpiled to Haxe via tools/ts2hx, then compiled back to TS via genes-ts, then typechecked and tested for behavioral parity. Keep it Node-only first; consider TSX later.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add one medium TS fixture with its own tests
- CI command performs original typecheck/test and roundtripped typecheck/test
- Output quality is validated (no pervasive any/unknown; isolated fallbacks documented)
- Runtime remains reasonable for CI
close_reason: Roundtrip fixture harness implemented end-to-end (snapshots + Haxe JS smoke + TS→Haxe→TS→JS parity) with typing guardrails; docs updated.

title: ts2hx: TypeScript/JS -> Haxe transpiler (future experiment)
description: Track future work on a TS/JS -> Haxe transpiler (aka reverse transpiler) intended to help migrate TS codebases into Haxe for multi-target portability. Not part of genes-ts v1.0.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented prototype exists
- A minimal spike translates 1-2 TS files
- Kept isolated so it cannot block genes-ts
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Initial ts2hx prototype is in place (tools/ts2hx), with docs/ts2hx/PLAN.md, fixture snapshot tests + Haxe JS smoke, and CI coverage via yarn test:ts2hx / genes-ts CI job.

title: Experiment: ts2hx (TypeScript/JS -> Haxe transpiler)
description: Low-priority experiment after genes-ts v1.0: transpile TypeScript to Haxe to enable easier porting of TS codebases to other targets. Likely implementation uses the TypeScript compiler API (Program + TypeChecker) to get resolved types and symbol info, then emits Haxe AST/text with extern-friendly boundaries. Consider writing this tool in TypeScript first (Node) to maximize reuse of TS typechecker.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documented prototype plan exists (inputs, supported TS subset, output style conventions)
- A minimal spike compiles 1-2 TS files to Haxe without runtime correctness claims
- Kept as separate tool/dir so it does not block genes-ts v1.0
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: ts2hx spike is implemented under tools/ts2hx with fixture snapshot tests + Haxe JS smoke. Wired into root scripts (yarn test:ts2hx + included in yarn test:ci) and CI (genes-ts job). Updated docs/ts2hx/PLAN.md + tools/ts2hx/README.md. Changes pushed.

title: Experiment: TS/JS → Haxe transpiler (ts2hx)
description: Low-priority post-1.0 experiment: build a TS/JS → Haxe source-to-source transpiler to help port existing TS/JS projects into Haxe (initially targeting JS via genes-ts, then enabling later manual refactors to port to other Haxe targets).
design: Canonical plan: docs/ts2hx/PLAN.md\n\nPositioning: post-1.0 migration tool (TS/JS → Haxe-for-JS first), not a full multi-target compiler. Use TS compiler API (Program + TypeChecker) for parsing + module resolution + symbols/TSX. See plan doc for rationale (including why TS is harder to transpile from vs Haxe).
acceptance_criteria:
- A short design doc describing scope + non-goals
- CLI can parse a small TS project (tsconfig) and emit compiling Haxe for a subset (imports/exports, functions, classes, interfaces/type aliases)
- Golden/snapshot fixtures for output stability
- Document the intended workflow + limitations
close_reason: All children completed

title: M8: Snapshot tests for generated TS output
description:
Add snapshot/golden tests for the emitted TypeScript to catch formatting/structural regressions that runtime tests might miss.

Scope ideas:
- Pick a small suite of fixtures (tests_ts/, tests_ts_full/, and/or curated haxe code)
- Emit TS into a temp dir
- Normalize output (stable formatting + newline)
- Compare against committed snapshots (per-module .ts files)
- Provide an update mode (e.g. UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1)
acceptance_criteria:
- npm run test:genes-ts and npm run test:genes-ts:minimal include snapshot checks
- Snapshots stored under tests_snapshots/ and are deterministic
- Update workflow: UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:genes-ts:snapshots
- Snapshot failures show readable git --no-index diffs
close_reason: Closed
